args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "classical-wiener-process-with-per-action-accumulators",
  "paradigm": "drift-diffusion-model",
  "name": "Classical Wiener Process with Per-Action Accumulators",
  "description": "Implements the canonical drift-diffusion model (Ratcliff, 1978) as a discrete-time Wiener process with independent parallel accumulators, one per candidate action. Each accumulator starts at a common starting point z0 and accumulates evidence at a drift rate computed from perceptual proximity to resources and past reward history, plus Gaussian noise. The first accumulator to cross the fixed upper boundary 'a' determines the chosen action (race model). If no accumulator crosses by T_max steps, the action with the highest accumulated evidence is selected. After each environment step, the reward history for the chosen action is updated via exponential moving average. This formulation directly instantiates postulates P1 (continuous evidence accumulation to threshold) and P2 (drift rate reflects stimulus quality) from Ratcliff (1978) and Bogacz et al. (2006).",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "X_i",
      "name": "evidence_accumulator",
      "description": "Running total of accumulated evidence favoring action i at internal time-step t. One accumulator per candidate action (6 total). Initialized at z0 each decision cycle.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.75,
      "range": [0, 1.5]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "v_i",
      "name": "drift_rate",
      "description": "Mean rate of evidence accumulation for action i, computed from perceptual proximity signal, eat-resource indicator, and reward history.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-5.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "xi_i",
      "name": "noise_sample",
      "description": "Independent Gaussian noise sample N(0,1) drawn at each internal integration step for each accumulator.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-5.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "R",
      "name": "chosen_action",
      "description": "The action whose accumulator first crosses threshold a, or the argmax accumulator at timeout.",
      "type": "str",
      "initial_value": "stay",
      "range": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "T_d",
      "name": "decision_time",
      "description": "Number of internal integration steps until a boundary crossing occurs (or T_max if timeout).",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": 0,
      "range": [1, 100]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_bar_i",
      "name": "reward_history",
      "description": "Exponentially-weighted running mean of past rewards received after taking action i. One value per action (6 total).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 5.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "a",
      "name": "decision_boundary",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Ratcliff & McKoon (2008); typical range [0.5, 2.0] for moderate caution"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma",
      "name": "diffusion_coefficient",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard scaling convention; Ratcliff (1978)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "z0",
      "name": "starting_point",
      "default": 0.75,
      "source": "Unbiased default = 0.5 * a; Ratcliff & Rouder (1998)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "dt",
      "name": "integration_timestep",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Discretization of Wiener process; Bogacz et al. (2006)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "T_max",
      "name": "max_deliberation_steps",
      "default": 100,
      "source": "Prevents indefinite accumulation; practical computational bound"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "k_res",
      "name": "resource_proximity_scaling",
      "default": 2.0,
      "source": "Derived from postulate P2: stimulus quality maps to drift rate (Ratcliff, 1978)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "k_eat",
      "name": "eat_action_base_drift",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Baseline desirability of eating when resource is present; linked to P2"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma",
      "name": "reward_history_decay",
      "default": 0.9,
      "source": "Standard exponential moving average decay; knowledge backbone default"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Drift rate computation: combines resource proximity signal, eat-resource indicator, and reward history into a per-action drift rate.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action i in [move_up, move_down, move_left, move_right, stay, eat]:\n  prox_i = 1.0 / (1.0 + manhattan_distance_to_nearest_food_in_direction(i))  # 0 if none visible\n  eat_bonus = k_eat if (i == 'eat' and food_on_current_cell) else 0.0\n  v_i = k_res * prox_i + eat_bonus + r_bar_i",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Evidence accumulation via discrete Wiener process: each accumulator evolves independently with drift and Gaussian noise.",
      "type": "SDE_discrete",
      "pseudocode": "X_i(t+1) = X_i(t) + v_i * dt + sigma * sqrt(dt) * random_normal(0, 1)\nX_i(t+1) = clamp(X_i(t+1), 0, a)  # reflecting lower boundary at 0, absorbing upper at a",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Boundary-crossing decision: the first accumulator to reach or exceed the threshold a determines the chosen action.",
      "type": "decision",
      "pseudocode": "for t in range(1, T_max + 1):\n  update all X_i via R2\n  winners = [i for i in actions if X_i(t) >= a]\n  if winners:\n    R = random_choice(winners)  # tie-breaking\n    T_d = t\n    break",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Timeout fallback: if no accumulator crosses the boundary within T_max steps, choose the action with the highest accumulator value.",
      "type": "decision",
      "pseudocode": "if T_d is None:  # no boundary crossing\n  R = argmax_i(X_i(T_max))\n  T_d = T_max",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Reward history update: after observing reward, update the exponentially-weighted reward mean for the chosen action. Non-chosen actions decay toward zero.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "r_bar_R = gamma * r_bar_R + (1 - gamma) * reward_observed\nfor i != R:\n  r_bar_i = gamma * r_bar_i  # decay non-chosen actions toward 0",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Per-step decision: compute drift rates from perception, run a stochastic race among 6 accumulators (one per action), and return the winning action. After receiving reward feedback, update reward history traces.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# Step 1: Parse perception",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_list = perception['resources'].get('food', [])",
      "food_on_cell = any(f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1] for f in food_list)",
      "",
      "# Step 2: Compute drift rates for each action via R1",
      "actions = ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right', 'stay', 'eat']",
      "direction_deltas = {'move_up': (0,-1), 'move_down': (0,1), 'move_left': (-1,0), 'move_right': (1,0)}",
      "for each action i:",
      "  if i in direction_deltas:",
      "    dx, dy = direction_deltas[i]",
      "    target = (pos[0]+dx, pos[1]+dy)",
      "    d_i = min Manhattan distance from target to any food (or large number if no food)",
      "    prox_i = 1.0 / (1.0 + d_i)",
      "  elif i == 'eat':",
      "    prox_i = 0.0  # eat uses k_eat bonus instead",
      "  elif i == 'stay':",
      "    prox_i = 0.0",
      "  eat_bonus = k_eat if (i == 'eat' and food_on_cell) else 0.0",
      "  v_i = k_res * prox_i + eat_bonus + r_bar[i]",
      "",
      "# Step 3: Initialize accumulators at z0",
      "X = {i: z0 for i in actions}",
      "",
      "# Step 4: Run stochastic race (R2, R3)",
      "chosen = None",
      "for t in range(1, T_max + 1):",
      "  for i in actions:",
      "    X[i] = X[i] + v_i * dt + sigma * sqrt(dt) * random_normal()",
      "    X[i] = clamp(X[i], 0, a)",
      "  winners = [i for i in actions if X[i] >= a]",
      "  if winners:",
      "    chosen = random_choice(winners)",
      "    break",
      "",
      "# Step 5: Timeout fallback (R4)",
      "if chosen is None:",
      "  chosen = argmax over actions of X[i]",
      "",
      "# Step 6: Return chosen action",
      "return Action(chosen)",
      "",
      "# Step 7 (in update method): Update reward history via R5",
      "r_bar[chosen] = gamma * r_bar[chosen] + (1 - gamma) * reward",
      "for i != chosen: r_bar[i] = gamma * r_bar[i]"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food']",
      "food_on_cell": "any(f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y for f in perception['resources']['food'])",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "step_number": "perception['step']"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect with reward 1.0; move/stay actions yield reward 0.0"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Agent navigates toward nearest food: drift rates for movement actions pointing toward food should dominate, causing those accumulators to win the race most often.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place food at (5,5), agent at (0,0) → over 50 steps, assert agent's mean distance to food decreases"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Agent eats when on food: when food is present on the agent's cell, the eat accumulator receives a strong k_eat drift bonus, making it likely to win the race.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent on food cell → run decide() 100 times → assert eat is chosen > 60% of the time"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Stochastic exploration: Gaussian noise in accumulators causes occasional non-optimal action choices, providing natural exploration without epsilon-greedy.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent equidistant from two food sources → run 100 decisions → assert both directions are chosen at least 20% each"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Reward history biases future decisions: after repeatedly receiving reward from eating, the eat action's reward trace increases, biasing the accumulator.",
      "test_pseudocode": "give agent 10 eat rewards → assert r_bar['eat'] > r_bar['move_up']"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Decision time varies with evidence quality: when one action has a much higher drift rate, the race resolves in fewer steps (lower T_d).",
      "test_pseudocode": "food directly adjacent (high prox) → mean T_d < T_d when food is far away"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Ratcliff, R. (1978). A theory of memory retrieval. Psychological Review, 85(2), 59-108. DOI: 10.1037/0033-295X.85.2.59",
    "Ratcliff, R., & Rouder, J. N. (1998). Modeling response times for two-choice decisions. Psychological Science, 9(5), 347-356. DOI: 10.1111/1467-9280.00067",
    "Ratcliff, R., & McKoon, G. (2008). The diffusion decision model: Theory and data for two-choice decision tasks. Neural Computation, 20(4), 873-922. DOI: 10.1162/neco.2008.12-06-420",
    "Bogacz, R., Brown, E., Moehlis, J., Holmes, P., & Cohen, J. D. (2006). The physics of optimal decision making. Psychological Review, 113(4), 700-765. DOI: 10.1037/0033-295X.113.4.700"
  ]
}

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "drive-reduction-ode-with-goal-directed-valuation",
  "paradigm": "homeostatic-regulation-of-food-valuation",
  "name": "Drive-Reduction ODE with Goal-Directed Valuation",
  "description": "A continuous-time homeostatic ODE system (discretized to grid steps) that couples an internal energy store to a sigmoid hunger drive, which in turn modulates multi-attribute food valuation. The agent uses deterministic argmax action selection over analytically computed action values that weight caloric value vs. effort cost depending on current hunger state. No learning is required — the agent is purely model-based and goal-directed, computing values from first principles each step. Grounded in Jacquier (2016) energy-balance ODEs and Rangel (2013) multi-attribute value computation framework (Postulates P1, P3, P6).",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "E",
      "name": "energy_store",
      "description": "Normalized internal energy reserve, proxy for fat mass. Depleted by basal metabolic cost each step and replenished by eating food.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "H",
      "name": "hunger_drive",
      "description": "Motivational drive state derived from energy deficit via sigmoid function. High when energy is below set-point, low when above.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_c",
      "name": "caloric_weight",
      "description": "State-dependent attribute weight for caloric/nutritional value of food. Equals hunger drive H — hungry agents weight calories highly.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_e",
      "name": "effort_weight",
      "description": "State-dependent attribute weight penalizing movement cost. Equals 1 - H — satiated agents are more effort-averse.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "d",
      "name": "resource_distance",
      "description": "Manhattan distance from agent's current position (or post-move position) to the nearest visible food resource.",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": 0,
      "range": [0, 18]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "ate",
      "name": "ate_flag",
      "description": "Binary indicator: 1 if the agent successfully consumed food on the previous step, 0 otherwise. Derived from last_action_result.",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": 0,
      "range": [0, 1]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_E",
      "name": "basal_metabolic_cost",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Jacquier (2016), adapted to grid timescale — represents continuous energy drain per step"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_food",
      "name": "energy_gain_from_eating",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Normalized; represents a substantial meal restoring ~30% of energy capacity (Jacquier 2016 energy intake scaling)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "E_set",
      "name": "energy_set_point",
      "default": 0.5,
      "source": "Jacquier (2016) homeostatic set-point concept — midpoint of normalized energy range"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "k_H",
      "name": "hunger_sensitivity",
      "default": 4.0,
      "source": "Tuned so sigmoid spans [0,1] meaningfully across the deficit range [-0.5, 0.5]"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_food",
      "name": "base_food_reward",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Rangel (2013) normalized value scale for food reward"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_step",
      "name": "step_cost",
      "default": -0.05,
      "source": "Small penalty for movement to encourage efficiency; negative value represents effort cost"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "theta",
      "name": "eat_threshold",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Rangel (2013) meal-initiation threshold concept — minimum hunger to initiate eating"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Energy dynamics: energy decreases by basal metabolic cost each step and increases when food is consumed.",
      "type": "difference_equation",
      "pseudocode": "E = clamp(E - alpha_E + c_food * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Hunger drive: sigmoid function of energy deficit from set-point. When energy is below set-point, hunger is high; when above, hunger is low.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "H = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-k_H * (E_set - E)))",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "State-dependent attribute weights: caloric weight equals hunger drive, effort weight is its complement. This captures the shift in food attribute weighting with homeostatic state.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "w_c = H\nw_e = 1.0 - H",
      "source_postulate": "P6"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Food value for eat action: product of caloric weight, base food reward, and hunger. Quadratic in H, so value rises steeply with hunger.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "V_eat = w_c * r_food * H  # equivalent to H^2 * r_food",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Move-toward-food value: caloric value discounted by distance to nearest food after the move, plus effort cost weighted by effort weight.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "V_move_a = w_c * r_food * H / (1.0 + d_after_move_a) + c_step * w_e",
      "source_postulate": "P6"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Stay value: baseline zero value, representing no gain and no cost from inaction.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "V_stay = 0.0",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Deterministic goal-directed action selection via argmax over analytically computed action values. The agent computes the value of every available action based on current hunger state and food positions, then greedily selects the highest-value action. A meal-initiation threshold prevents eating when hunger is below a minimum level, implementing Rangel (2013) threshold concept.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# 1. Update internal state from previous step",
      "ate = 1 if last_action_result indicates successful consumption else 0",
      "E = clamp(E - alpha_E + c_food * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "H = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-k_H * (E_set - E)))",
      "w_c = H",
      "w_e = 1.0 - H",
      "",
      "# 2. Compute distances to nearest food from each candidate position",
      "food_list = perception['resources']['food']",
      "nearest_food_dist = manhattan_distance_to_nearest(agent_pos, food_list)",
      "food_at_cell = any(f for f in food_list if f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y)",
      "",
      "# 3. Compute action values",
      "action_values = {}",
      "",
      "if food_at_cell and H >= theta:",
      "    action_values['eat'] = w_c * r_food * H",
      "elif food_at_cell and H < theta:",
      "    action_values['eat'] = 0.0  # below meal-initiation threshold",
      "",
      "for move_action in ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right']:",
      "    new_pos = apply_move(agent_pos, move_action, grid_width, grid_height)",
      "    d_after = manhattan_distance_to_nearest(new_pos, food_list)",
      "    action_values[move_action] = w_c * r_food * H / (1.0 + d_after) + c_step * w_e",
      "",
      "action_values['stay'] = 0.0",
      "",
      "# 4. Select action with highest value (ties broken randomly)",
      "best_action = argmax(action_values)  # random tiebreak",
      "return Action(best_action)"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food']  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}",
      "ate_flag": "1 if perception['last_action_result'] indicates eat success (resource consumed) else 0",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "resource_distance": "min(|agent.x - f.x| + |agent.y - f.y| for f in food_sources) or grid_width + grid_height if no food"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect with reward 1.0; move/stay actions → reward 0.0 from env. Internal energy/hunger dynamics are tracked separately."
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Hunger increases without eating: as energy depletes from basal metabolism, hunger drive rises via sigmoid function.",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 50 steps without food available → assert H increases monotonically toward 1.0"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Agent seeks food when hungry: when H > theta and food is visible, agent moves toward nearest food.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set E = 0.2 (H ≈ 0.88) with food at distance 3 → assert agent selects move action reducing distance to food"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Agent eats when on food and hungry: food at cell + high hunger → eat action selected.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent on food cell with E = 0.2 → assert agent selects 'eat'"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Agent stays or wanders when satiated: when energy is at or above set-point, hunger is low and agent prefers staying over movement.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set E = 0.8 (H ≈ 0.12) → assert agent selects 'stay' or moves away from food (V_stay = 0 > V_move when H is very low)"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Meal-initiation threshold prevents eating when not hungry enough.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent on food cell with E = 0.7 (H ≈ 0.27 < theta=0.3) → assert agent does NOT select 'eat'"
    },
    {
      "id": "B6",
      "description": "Energy oscillates around set-point over time as agent cycles between eating and depleting.",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 500 steps with food available → assert mean(E) is close to E_set (within ±0.15)"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Jacquier, M. (2016). Mathematical modeling of the hormonal regulation of food intake and body weight. Doctoral thesis, Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1. HAL: tel-01273347",
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357"
  ]
}

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "rescorlawagner-cached-value-agent-with-softmax-action-selection",
  "paradigm": "pavlovian-control-of-food-approach",
  "name": "Rescorla–Wagner Cached-Value Agent with Softmax Action Selection",
  "description": "A Pavlovian agent that learns cached state values V(s) for each grid cell via the Rescorla–Wagner (TD(0)) prediction-error rule, then selects actions stochastically via softmax over hunger-modulated Pavlovian action values. The agent does NOT learn action–outcome mappings (no Q(s,a) table); instead, action values are derived from the Pavlovian values of destination states, implementing the 'prepared behavior' constraint (P3). Approach is hardwired toward high-value states, and hunger multiplicatively gates the effective value, capturing state-dependent Pavlovian responding (P4).",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "V",
      "name": "pavlovian_state_values",
      "description": "Dictionary mapping grid cell (x,y) to cached expected reward. Represents the learned CS–US associative strength for each location.",
      "type": "dict[tuple[int,int], float]",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "delta_t",
      "name": "reward_prediction_error",
      "description": "Signed difference between received reward and predicted value at time t. Positive when reward exceeds expectation, negative otherwise. Encodes the dopaminergic RPE signal.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-2.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "h_t",
      "name": "hunger_level",
      "description": "Internal deprivation state normalized to [0,1]. Increases each step by alpha_h and decreases by gamma_sat upon eating. Modulates Pavlovian value gain.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_t",
      "name": "reward_received",
      "description": "Scalar food reward obtained at time t. 0 if no food eaten, r_food if food consumed via eat action.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "Q_Pav",
      "name": "pavlovian_action_values",
      "description": "Effective value of each action under Pavlovian control, computed as hunger-modulated V(s_a) minus movement cost. Not stored persistently; recomputed each step.",
      "type": "dict[str, float]",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-Infinity, Infinity]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "P_a",
      "name": "action_probabilities",
      "description": "Softmax probability distribution over available actions. Recomputed each step from Q_Pav values.",
      "type": "dict[str, float]",
      "initial_value": null,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "alpha",
      "name": "learning_rate",
      "default": 0.15,
      "source": "Empirically validated range 0.01–0.5 for Pavlovian conditioning; Rescorla & Wagner (1972) as cited in Rangel (2013)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Standard softmax exploration parameter; validated in prior pipeline runs"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_food",
      "name": "food_reward_magnitude",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Matches env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_h",
      "name": "hunger_increment_rate",
      "default": 0.02,
      "source": "Per-step hunger growth reflecting metabolic demand; calibrated for 10x10 grid"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma_sat",
      "name": "satiation_decrement",
      "default": 0.20,
      "source": "Hunger reduction upon eating; calibrated so ~5 meals achieve full satiation from h=1.0"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu",
      "name": "hunger_value_gain",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Wanting–hunger coupling; Berridge & Robinson (2003) as cited in Rangel (2013)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_step",
      "name": "movement_cost",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Small energetic cost of locomotion to break ties against aimless movement"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "R_max",
      "name": "maximum_value",
      "default": 2.0,
      "source": "Upper bound on V(s) for numerical stability; 2x the maximum single-step reward"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Reward prediction error: the signed difference between received reward and the cached Pavlovian value of the current cell.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "delta_t = r_t - V.get((x, y), 0.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Pavlovian value update via Rescorla–Wagner rule: V(s) is incremented proportionally to the prediction error, clipped to [0, R_max].",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "V[(x, y)] = clip(V.get((x, y), 0.0) + alpha * delta_t, 0.0, R_max)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Hunger dynamics: hunger increases by alpha_h each step and decreases by gamma_sat when the agent eats, clipped to [0, 1].",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "ate = 1.0 if (action == 'eat' and eat_succeeded) else 0.0\nh_t = clip(h_t + alpha_h - gamma_sat * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Hunger-modulated Pavlovian action value: each action's value is the hunger-scaled V of its destination state, minus a small movement cost for movement actions.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a in available_actions:\n  if a == 'eat' and food_at_position:\n    Q_Pav[a] = mu * h_t * V.get((x, y), 0.0)\n  elif a == 'eat' and not food_at_position:\n    Q_Pav[a] = -1e9  # unavailable\n  elif a == 'stay':\n    Q_Pav[a] = mu * h_t * V.get((x, y), 0.0)\n  elif a is movement to valid cell (x_a, y_a):\n    Q_Pav[a] = mu * h_t * V.get((x_a, y_a), 0.0) - c_step\n  elif a is movement to invalid cell (out of bounds):\n    Q_Pav[a] = -1e9  # unavailable",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Softmax action selection: action probabilities are proportional to exp(beta * Q_Pav(a)), implementing stochastic Pavlovian approach biased toward high-value states.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "max_q = max(Q_Pav.values())  # for numerical stability\nfor a in available_actions:\n  P_a[a] = exp(beta * (Q_Pav[a] - max_q))\nZ = sum(P_a.values())\nfor a in available_actions:\n  P_a[a] = P_a[a] / Z\nselected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=P_a)",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P3"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Pavlovian agent decision cycle: perceive environment, compute destination-state values for all actions, apply hunger modulation, select action via softmax, then update cached values and hunger after observing outcome. The agent has no model of action-outcome contingencies — it only learns state values V(s) and is hardwired to approach high-V states.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# 1. PERCEIVE",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_list = perception['resources'].get('food', [])",
      "food_positions = set((f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list)",
      "food_at_position = pos in food_positions",
      "grid_w, grid_h = perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height']",
      "",
      "# 2. COMPUTE DESTINATION CELLS for each action",
      "destinations = {",
      "  'move_up':    (pos[0], pos[1] - 1),",
      "  'move_down':  (pos[0], pos[1] + 1),",
      "  'move_left':  (pos[0] - 1, pos[1]),",
      "  'move_right': (pos[0] + 1, pos[1]),",
      "  'stay':       pos,",
      "  'eat':        pos",
      "}",
      "",
      "# 3. COMPUTE Q_Pav(a) for each action (R4)",
      "Q_Pav = {}",
      "for action_name, dest in destinations.items():",
      "  if action_name == 'eat':",
      "    if food_at_position:",
      "      Q_Pav['eat'] = mu * h_t * V.get(pos, 0.0)",
      "    else:",
      "      Q_Pav['eat'] = -1e9",
      "  elif action_name == 'stay':",
      "    Q_Pav['stay'] = mu * h_t * V.get(pos, 0.0)",
      "  else:  # movement action",
      "    dx, dy = dest",
      "    if 0 <= dx < grid_w and 0 <= dy < grid_h:",
      "      Q_Pav[action_name] = mu * h_t * V.get(dest, 0.0) - c_step",
      "    else:",
      "      Q_Pav[action_name] = -1e9",
      "",
      "# 4. SOFTMAX ACTION SELECTION (R5)",
      "max_q = max(Q_Pav.values())",
      "exp_vals = {a: exp(beta * (q - max_q)) for a, q in Q_Pav.items()}",
      "Z = sum(exp_vals.values())",
      "P_a = {a: v / Z for a, v in exp_vals.items()}",
      "selected_action = random_choice(list(P_a.keys()), weights=list(P_a.values()))",
      "",
      "# 5. RETURN action",
      "return Action(selected_action)"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food'] — list of dicts with 'x', 'y', 'type', 'palatability'",
      "food_at_position": "any(f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y'] for f in perception['resources']['food'])",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "ate_food": "last_action_result — check if eat action succeeded (consumed == true)",
      "reward_received": "reward argument in update() method; 1.0 from ConsumeEffect on successful eat"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions yield reward = 0.0"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Hunger increases without eating: hunger grows by alpha_h=0.02 per step toward 1.0",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 50 steps without food available → assert h_t > initial_h_t and h_t approx initial_h_t + 50*0.02*(1 accounting for clip)"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Value learning at food locations: after eating at a cell, V(s) increases toward r_food=1.0",
      "test_pseudocode": "place food at (5,5), agent eats 10 times at (5,5) → assert V[(5,5)] > 0.8"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Approach bias toward high-V cells: agent preferentially moves toward cells with higher learned V values",
      "test_pseudocode": "set V[(3,3)]=1.0, V[(7,7)]=0.0, agent at (5,5) → over 100 trials, assert more move_left/move_up chosen than move_right/move_down"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Hunger modulates approach: higher hunger increases probability of approach actions to food-rich cells",
      "test_pseudocode": "set h_t=0.1 vs h_t=0.9, same V map → assert P(approach_food) is higher when h_t=0.9"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Extinction: when food is removed from a previously rewarded cell, V(s) decreases over time",
      "test_pseudocode": "train V[(5,5)]=1.0, then agent visits (5,5) 20 times without food → assert V[(5,5)] < 0.5"
    },
    {
      "id": "B6",
      "description": "Softmax stochasticity: with moderate beta, agent occasionally explores non-optimal directions",
      "test_pseudocode": "set clear value gradient, run 100 decisions → assert at least some non-greedy actions selected"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357",
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Rescorla, R. A., & Wagner, A. R. (1972). A theory of Pavlovian conditioning: Variations in the effectiveness of reinforcement and nonreinforcement. As cited in Rangel (2013)."
  ]
}

{
  "formulation_id": "weighted-linear-summation-with-state-dependent-attribute-weights-algebraic",
  "paradigm": "attribute-based-value-computation",
  "name": "Weighted Linear Summation with State-Dependent Attribute Weights (Algebraic)",
  "description": "Static algebraic multi-attribute utility model where overall option value is a weighted linear sum of attribute values. Each food resource is decomposed into two attributes: an immediate attribute (proximity-based desirability) and an abstract attribute (palatability/quality). Attribute weights are dynamically modulated by internal hunger state and learnable attentional allocations. Actions are selected via softmax over computed action values. Attention allocations update via reward prediction error, enabling the agent to learn which attribute dimension is more predictive of reward. Grounded in Rangel (2013) postulates P1 (weighted linear summation), P3 (attentional modulation of weights), and P5 (immediate vs. abstract attribute distinction).",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "V_o",
      "name": "option_value",
      "description": "Scalar subjective value of an option (resource), computed as weighted sum of attribute values. Proximate driver of action selection.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "a_imm",
      "name": "immediate_attribute_value",
      "description": "Attribute value for the immediate/proximity dimension. For movement actions: 1.0 - (manhattan_distance / max_distance), where max_distance = grid_width + grid_height - 2. For eat action on a resource: 1.0. For stay or actions with no reachable resource: 0.0.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "a_abs",
      "name": "abstract_attribute_value",
      "description": "Attribute value for the abstract/quality dimension. Mapped from the 'palatability' property of the nearest food resource targeted by the action, normalized to [-1, 1] via: a_abs = 2 * (palatability - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1. If no resource is targeted, a_abs = 0.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_imm",
      "name": "immediate_weight",
      "description": "Effective weight for the immediate attribute, computed from attentional allocation and hunger state. Always in [0, 1] and sums with w_abs to 1.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_abs",
      "name": "abstract_weight",
      "description": "Effective weight for the abstract attribute, computed from attentional allocation and temporal discount. Always in [0, 1] and sums with w_imm to 1.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "H_t",
      "name": "hunger",
      "description": "Internal physiological drive state. Rises by eta each step, drops by R_food when eating. 0 = fully sated, 1 = maximally hungry.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_imm",
      "name": "attention_immediate",
      "description": "Attentional allocation to the immediate attribute. Learned via reward prediction error. Sum of alpha_imm + alpha_abs = 1.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.05, 0.95]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_abs",
      "name": "attention_abstract",
      "description": "Attentional allocation to the abstract attribute. Complement of alpha_imm. Learned via reward prediction error.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.05, 0.95]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V_a",
      "name": "action_value",
      "description": "Value of taking a specific action, derived from the option value of the resource it targets.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "softmax_inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Prior pipeline calibrations; typical range 1-10"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma_H",
      "name": "hunger_influence_on_immediate_weight",
      "default": 0.6,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): hunger amplifies taste/hedonic weight"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "delta",
      "name": "temporal_discount_for_abstract_attributes",
      "default": 0.7,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): abstract attributes are down-weighted under low control"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "eta",
      "name": "hunger_rise_rate",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Simulation convention (slow metabolic timescale)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "R_food",
      "name": "hunger_restoration_from_eating",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Simulation convention"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "lam",
      "name": "attention_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.05,
      "source": "Standard reinforcement learning rate for attentional updates"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "epsilon",
      "name": "minimum_attention",
      "default": 0.05,
      "source": "Prevents any attribute from being fully ignored (Rangel 2013: all attributes contribute)"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Compute effective attribute weights from hunger state and attentional allocation. Hunger amplifies the immediate attribute weight; the abstract attribute is discounted by delta. Weights are normalized to sum to 1.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "unnorm_imm = alpha_imm * (1 + gamma_H * H_t)\nunnorm_abs = alpha_abs * delta\nZ = unnorm_imm + unnorm_abs\nw_imm = unnorm_imm / Z\nw_abs = unnorm_abs / Z",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P3, P5"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Compute attribute values for each candidate action. Immediate attribute = proximity-based desirability. Abstract attribute = palatability-derived quality rating of the targeted resource.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "max_dist = grid_width + grid_height - 2\nfor each action a in available_actions:\n  if a == 'eat':\n    if food_at_agent_position:\n      a_imm = 1.0\n      a_abs = 2.0 * (food.palatability - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0\n    else:\n      a_imm = 0.0\n      a_abs = 0.0\n  elif a == 'stay':\n    a_imm = 0.0\n    a_abs = 0.0\n  else:  # movement action\n    target_pos = position + direction_delta(a)\n    nearest_food = find_nearest_food_from(target_pos, food_list)\n    if nearest_food is not None:\n      dist = manhattan_distance(target_pos, nearest_food)\n      a_imm = 1.0 - dist / max_dist\n      a_abs = 2.0 * (nearest_food.palatability - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0\n    else:\n      a_imm = 0.0\n      a_abs = 0.0",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Compute option value as weighted linear sum of attribute values.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "V_a = w_imm * a_imm + w_abs * a_abs",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Softmax action selection over action values.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a:\n  logit_a = beta * V_a\nmax_logit = max(logits)\nfor each action a:\n  prob_a = exp(logit_a - max_logit)\nprob = prob / sum(prob)\nselected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=prob)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Hunger state update. Hunger rises by eta each step and drops by R_food when eating. Clipped to [0, 1].",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "ate = 1 if (action == 'eat' and last_action_result.consumed) else 0\nH_t = clip(H_t + eta - R_food * ate, 0, 1)",
      "source_postulate": "P5"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Attention allocation update via reward prediction error. Attributes that are more correlated with positive RPE gain attention. Attention floored at epsilon and renormalized.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "RPE = reward - V_selected\nalpha_imm = alpha_imm + lam * RPE * a_imm_selected\nalpha_abs = alpha_abs + lam * RPE * a_abs_selected\nalpha_imm = max(epsilon, alpha_imm)\nalpha_abs = max(epsilon, alpha_abs)\ntotal = alpha_imm + alpha_abs\nalpha_imm = alpha_imm / total\nalpha_abs = alpha_abs / total",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Each simulation step: (1) Read perception to get position, food locations, and hunger. (2) Compute attribute weights from hunger and attention via R1. (3) For each of the 6 available actions, compute immediate and abstract attribute values via R2. (4) Compute action values via R3. (5) Select action via softmax (R4). After action execution: (6) Update hunger via R5. (7) Update attention allocations via R6 using the reward prediction error.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# --- DECIDE PHASE ---",
      "food_list = perception['resources']['food']",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "grid_w = perception['grid_width']",
      "grid_h = perception['grid_height']",
      "",
      "# R1: Compute attribute weights",
      "unnorm_imm = alpha_imm * (1 + gamma_H * H_t)",
      "unnorm_abs = alpha_abs * delta",
      "Z = unnorm_imm + unnorm_abs",
      "w_imm = unnorm_imm / Z",
      "w_abs = unnorm_abs / Z",
      "",
      "# R2 + R3: For each action, compute attribute values and action value",
      "action_values = {}",
      "action_attributes = {}",
      "max_dist = grid_w + grid_h - 2",
      "food_at_pos = [f for f in food_list if f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1]]",
      "",
      "for action_name in ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right', 'stay', 'eat']:",
      "  if action_name == 'eat':",
      "    if food_at_pos:",
      "      a_imm = 1.0",
      "      a_abs = 2.0 * (food_at_pos[0]['palatability'] - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0",
      "    else:",
      "      a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0",
      "  elif action_name == 'stay':",
      "    a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0",
      "  else:",
      "    dx, dy = direction_delta(action_name)  # move_up: (0,-1), etc.",
      "    nx = clip(pos[0]+dx, 0, grid_w-1)",
      "    ny = clip(pos[1]+dy, 0, grid_h-1)",
      "    nearest = find_nearest_food((nx, ny), food_list)",
      "    if nearest:",
      "      dist = abs(nx - nearest['x']) + abs(ny - nearest['y'])",
      "      a_imm = 1.0 - dist / max_dist",
      "      a_abs = 2.0 * (nearest['palatability'] - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0",
      "    else:",
      "      a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0",
      "  V_a = w_imm * a_imm + w_abs * a_abs",
      "  action_values[action_name] = V_a",
      "  action_attributes[action_name] = (a_imm, a_abs)",
      "",
      "# R4: Softmax action selection",
      "selected_action = softmax_sample(action_values, beta)",
      "store selected action's V_a, a_imm, a_abs for update phase",
      "",
      "# --- UPDATE PHASE (called after action execution) ---",
      "# R5: Hunger update",
      "ate = 1 if (selected_action == 'eat' and reward > 0) else 0",
      "H_t = clip(H_t + eta - R_food * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "",
      "# R6: Attention update via RPE",
      "RPE = reward - V_selected",
      "alpha_imm = alpha_imm + lam * RPE * a_imm_selected",
      "alpha_abs = alpha_abs + lam * RPE * a_abs_selected",
      "alpha_imm = max(epsilon, alpha_imm)",
      "alpha_abs = max(epsilon, alpha_abs)",
      "total = alpha_imm + alpha_abs",
      "alpha_imm = alpha_imm / total",
      "alpha_abs = alpha_abs / total"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food']  # list of {'x': int, 'y': int, 'type': 'food', 'palatability': float}",
      "grid_dims": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "ate_food": "last_action_result.get('consumed', False) or (reward > 0 and action == 'eat')",
      "food_palatability": "resource['palatability'] for each resource in food_sources"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action -> ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 on successful consumption)"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Hunger increases over time without eating, biasing the agent toward proximity-seeking (immediate attribute) behavior.",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 50 steps with no food reachable -> assert H_t > 0.9; assert w_imm > w_abs"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "After eating, hunger drops and the agent becomes relatively more quality-sensitive (abstract attribute weight increases).",
      "test_pseudocode": "eat food at step T -> assert H_{T+1} < H_T; assert w_abs at T+1 > w_abs at T (relative to w_imm)"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Agent preferentially moves toward closer food (high immediate attribute) when very hungry.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set H_t = 0.95, place two foods: near (dist=1, low palatability) and far (dist=5, high palatability) -> agent more likely to move toward near food"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Attention allocation shifts toward the attribute dimension that better predicts reward over time.",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 200 steps in env where high-palatability food gives reward -> assert alpha_abs increases relative to initial value"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Agent eats food when standing on it and hungry.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent on food, set H_t = 0.8 -> assert P(eat) is highest action probability"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717-1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9(7), 545-556. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357"
  ]
}

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "dual-q-table-with-fixed-exponential-decay-arbitration",
  "paradigm": "goal-directed-vs-habitual-control",
  "name": "Dual Q-Table with Fixed Exponential-Decay Arbitration",
  "description": "Algebraic dual-system reinforcement learning agent maintaining two separate Q-tables: a model-free (habitual) Q-table updated by TD(0) and a model-based (goal-directed) Q-table computed prospectively from a learned transition model and hunger-modulated outcome desirability. The two value streams are blended via a deterministic arbitration weight that decays exponentially with state visit count, implementing the overtraining-to-habit shift (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008; postulate P4). Early in training, goal-directed control dominates; after extensive exposure, habitual cached values take over. Outcome devaluation (e.g., satiation reducing hunger) immediately affects model-based values but not model-free values, reproducing the key behavioral signature of goal-directed vs. habitual control (postulate P5).",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "s",
      "name": "state",
      "description": "Agent's current grid cell encoded as (x, y) tuple",
      "type": "discrete",
      "initial_value": "from perception (x, y)",
      "range": ["(0,0)", "(grid_width-1, grid_height-1)"]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "Q_MF",
      "name": "model_free_q_table",
      "description": "Cached habitual action values. Dictionary mapping (state, action) pairs to scalar values, updated incrementally by TD(0) prediction errors. Insensitive to outcome desirability changes (postulate P3).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "Q_MB",
      "name": "model_based_q_value",
      "description": "Goal-directed action values computed prospectively each step from the learned transition model and current hunger-modulated desirability. Not cached — recomputed every decision step (postulate P2).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "Q_net",
      "name": "net_action_value",
      "description": "Arbitrated blend of Q_MF and Q_MB, weighted by omega. Used for final action selection via softmax.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "h",
      "name": "hunger_drive",
      "description": "Internal physiological state representing current need. Rises each step without eating, drops upon consuming food. Modulates goal-directed outcome desirability r_D (postulate P2, assumption: state-dependent desirability).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_D",
      "name": "decision_time_desirability",
      "description": "Current subjective value of each possible outcome, modulated by hunger h. For food outcomes: r_D = h * r_food. For non-food outcomes: r_D = c_step.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "p_hat",
      "name": "learned_transition_model",
      "description": "Agent's estimate of action-outcome contingency p_hat(o|a,s). Dictionary mapping (state, action, outcome) triples to probabilities. Core element of goal-directed system (postulate P2).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "delta",
      "name": "td_prediction_error",
      "description": "Temporal difference prediction error: delta = r_received + gamma * max_a' Q_MF(s', a') - Q_MF(s, a). Drives model-free learning (Rangel et al., 2008).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "omega",
      "name": "arbitration_weight",
      "description": "Proportion of goal-directed control in the value mixture. Computed as exp(-lambda * N(s)), decaying from 1.0 (full GD) toward 0.0 (full habit) as state visit count increases (postulate P4).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 1.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "N",
      "name": "visit_count",
      "description": "Number of times the agent has visited each state s. Dictionary mapping states to integer counts. Primary driver of the arbitration weight decay.",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": 0,
      "range": [0, "infinity"]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_MF",
      "name": "model_free_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.10,
      "source": "Standard TD-learning range (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_T",
      "name": "transition_model_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.20,
      "source": "Higher than alpha_MF — model-based systems update faster (Rangel, 2013; postulate P2)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma",
      "name": "temporal_discount_factor",
      "default": 0.95,
      "source": "Standard RL discounting (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Moderate exploitation level (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "lambda_habit",
      "name": "habit_formation_rate",
      "default": 0.05,
      "source": "Controls speed of exponential decay of omega; tuned to match overtraining timeline (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008; postulate P4)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "eta",
      "name": "hunger_rise_rate",
      "default": 0.02,
      "source": "Per-step increase when not eating; biologically motivated hunger accumulation"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "phi",
      "name": "satiation_amount",
      "default": 0.30,
      "source": "Hunger reduction upon eating; provides meaningful satiation per food event"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_food",
      "name": "base_food_reward",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Normalized reward unit matching env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_step",
      "name": "step_cost",
      "default": -0.01,
      "source": "Small penalty to encourage efficiency and discourage aimless wandering"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Hunger dynamics: hunger rises each step by eta and drops by phi when food is consumed. Clipped to [0, 1].",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "h = clip(h + eta - phi * ate_food, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "Assumption: state-dependent desirability; internal hunger modulates goal-directed valuation"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Decision-time desirability: subjective value of food outcome is hunger-modulated; non-food outcome incurs step cost.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "r_D_food = h * r_food\nr_D_nofood = c_step",
      "source_postulate": "P2 — goal-directed value uses current desirability r^D(o|s)"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Model-based Q-value computation (prospective). For eat action at food location: sum over outcomes of p_hat(o|eat,s) * r_D(o). For move actions: c_step + gamma * max_a' Q_MF(s', a') where s' is the destination cell. For eat with no food: c_step. For stay: c_step.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a:\n  if a == 'eat' and food_at_position:\n    Q_MB[s][a] = p_hat['food'|'eat',s] * r_D_food + (1 - p_hat['food'|'eat',s]) * r_D_nofood\n  elif a in ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right']:\n    s_next = compute_next_state(s, a)\n    Q_MB[s][a] = c_step + gamma * max(Q_MF[s_next][a_prime] for a_prime in actions)\n  else:\n    Q_MB[s][a] = c_step",
      "source_postulate": "P2 — V_GD(a|s) = sum_o p(o|a,s) * r^D(o|s)"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Transition model update: after observing outcome o_obs from action a in state s, update p_hat toward the observed outcome using learning rate alpha_T.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "p_hat[o_obs|a,s] = p_hat[o_obs|a,s] + alpha_T * (1.0 - p_hat[o_obs|a,s])\nfor o != o_obs:\n  p_hat[o|a,s] = p_hat[o|a,s] + alpha_T * (0.0 - p_hat[o|a,s])",
      "source_postulate": "P2 — action-outcome contingency p(o|a,s) is learned and updated from experience"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "TD prediction error: computed after observing reward r_received and transitioning to new state s'.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "delta = r_received + gamma * max(Q_MF[s_next][a_prime] for a_prime in actions) - Q_MF[s][a]",
      "source_postulate": "P3 — habitual system uses TD learning; prediction error delta drives cached value updates"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Model-free Q-value update: incremental TD(0) update using the prediction error.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "Q_MF[s][a] = Q_MF[s][a] + alpha_MF * delta",
      "source_postulate": "P3 — V_H(a|s) updated via incremental experience, not decomposed into contingency and desirability"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Arbitration weight: exponential decay based on state visit count. omega=1 at zero visits (full goal-directed), decays toward 0 (full habitual) with training.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "omega = exp(-lambda_habit * N[s])",
      "source_postulate": "P4 — goal-directed control predominates early; habitual control predominates after extensive training"
    },
    {
      "id": "R8",
      "description": "Net action value: linear blend of model-based and model-free Q-values weighted by arbitration parameter omega.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "Q_net[s][a] = omega * Q_MB[s][a] + (1 - omega) * Q_MF[s][a]",
      "source_postulate": "P1 — two dissociable valuation systems compete/cooperate via mixing"
    },
    {
      "id": "R9",
      "description": "Softmax action selection: probability of each action proportional to exponentiated net Q-value, controlled by inverse temperature beta.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "probs = softmax(beta * Q_net[s][a] for a in actions)\nselected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=probs)",
      "source_postulate": "Assumption: common currency of value enters common action-selection mechanism"
    },
    {
      "id": "R10",
      "description": "Visit count increment: after each step in state s, increment the visit counter.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "N[s] = N[s] + 1",
      "source_postulate": "P4 — training trial count is primary driver of habit formation"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Each step: (1) Read current state s=(x,y), list of food resources, hunger h. (2) Compute hunger-modulated desirability r_D for food vs non-food outcomes (R2). (3) For each action, compute model-based Q_MB prospectively using learned transition model and r_D (R3). (4) Look up cached model-free Q_MF values for current state (R6 applied in prior steps). (5) Compute arbitration weight omega from visit count N(s) via exponential decay (R7). (6) Blend into Q_net = omega*Q_MB + (1-omega)*Q_MF (R8). (7) Select action via softmax over Q_net (R9). (8) After execution: update hunger (R1), transition model (R4), compute TD error (R5), update Q_MF (R6), increment N(s) (R10).",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# === DECIDE ===",
      "s = (perception.x, perception.y)",
      "food_positions = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in perception.resources.get('food', [])]",
      "food_here = s in food_positions",
      "",
      "# R2: Compute desirability",
      "r_D_food = h * r_food",
      "r_D_nofood = c_step",
      "",
      "# R3: Compute model-based Q for each action",
      "for a in actions:",
      "  if a == 'eat' and food_here:",
      "    Q_MB[s][a] = p_hat.get((s,a,'food'), 0.5) * r_D_food + (1 - p_hat.get((s,a,'food'), 0.5)) * r_D_nofood",
      "  elif a in ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right']:",
      "    s_next = clamp_to_grid(apply_move(s, a))",
      "    Q_MB[s][a] = c_step + gamma * max(Q_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)",
      "  else:  # stay or eat with no food",
      "    Q_MB[s][a] = c_step",
      "",
      "# R7: Arbitration weight from visit count",
      "omega = exp(-lambda_habit * N.get(s, 0))",
      "",
      "# R8: Blend",
      "for a in actions:",
      "  Q_net[a] = omega * Q_MB[s][a] + (1 - omega) * Q_MF.get((s, a), 0.0)",
      "",
      "# R9: Softmax selection",
      "probs = softmax([beta * Q_net[a] for a in actions])",
      "return random_choice(actions, probs)",
      "",
      "# === UPDATE (after action execution) ===",
      "ate_food = (action == 'eat' and last_action_result.get('consumed', False))",
      "r_received = r_food if ate_food else c_step",
      "",
      "# R1: Update hunger",
      "h = clip(h + eta - phi * int(ate_food), 0.0, 1.0)",
      "",
      "# R4: Update transition model",
      "o_obs = 'food' if ate_food else 'nofood'",
      "p_hat[(s, action, o_obs)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5) + alpha_T * (1.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5))",
      "for o in outcomes where o != o_obs:",
      "  p_hat[(s, action, o)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5) + alpha_T * (0.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5))",
      "",
      "# R5-R6: TD update for model-free Q",
      "s_next = (new_perception.x, new_perception.y)",
      "delta = r_received + gamma * max(Q_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions) - Q_MF.get((s, action), 0.0)",
      "Q_MF[(s, action)] = Q_MF.get((s, action), 0.0) + alpha_MF * delta",
      "",
      "# R10: Increment visit count",
      "N[s] = N.get(s, 0) + 1"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "state_s": "(perception.x, perception.y)",
      "food_positions": "perception.resources.food — list of {x, y, type, palatability} dicts",
      "food_at_current_position": "any(f['x'] == perception.x and f['y'] == perception.y for f in perception.resources.get('food', []))",
      "grid_bounds": "(perception.grid_width, perception.grid_height)",
      "step_number": "perception.step",
      "ate_food": "action was 'eat' and perception.last_action_result indicates consumption"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action at food location → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions → internal step cost c_step = -0.01"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Goal-directed dominance in novel states: when visiting a state for the first time (N(s)=0), omega=1.0 so the agent relies entirely on model-based prospective computation, choosing actions that lead toward food based on hunger-modulated desirability.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Place agent in new state with food nearby → assert omega == 1.0 → assert agent moves toward food when hungry"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Habit formation via overtraining: after many visits to the same state, omega decays toward 0 and the agent's behavior is dominated by cached Q_MF values regardless of current hunger level.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Run agent for 200 steps in same area → assert omega < 0.1 for frequently visited states → assert Q_net approximately equals Q_MF"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Devaluation sensitivity (early training): when the agent is sated (h≈0) and in a novel state (high omega), it should avoid eating because Q_MB reflects the low current desirability of food.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Set h=0.01, N(s)=0 → compute Q_MB for eat → assert Q_MB_eat ≈ 0.01 (very low) → assert agent prefers non-eat actions"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Devaluation insensitivity (late training): when the agent is sated but in an overtrained state (low omega), it may still eat because Q_MF reflects historically high rewards from eating.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Set h=0.01, N(s)=100 → assert omega < 0.01 → Q_net dominated by Q_MF → if Q_MF[eat] was high from past experience, agent still eats"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Hunger drives food-seeking: with high hunger (h≈1.0) and food available, the goal-directed system assigns high value to eating, producing directed movement toward food or immediate eating.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Set h=0.9, food at current position → assert Q_MB[eat] ≈ 0.9 → assert eat is most probable action"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9, 545–556.",
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724."
  ]
}

{
  "formulation_id": "attribute-reweighting-algebraic-model",
  "paradigm": "dlpfc-self-control-modulation",
  "name": "Attribute-Reweighting Algebraic Model (Rangel variant)",
  "description": "Algebraic weighted-sum valuation model where dlPFC dynamically modulates the relative weights of hedonic (taste/palatability) vs. abstract (health) attributes in vmPFC composite chosen value computation. Conflict between immediate and long-term preferences triggers dlPFC engagement, which shifts attribute weights toward the health-beneficial option. A depletion mechanism limits sustained self-control. Action selection uses softmax over composite chosen values. Based on Rangel (2013) postulates P1 and P3: the vmPFC computes a composite value by summing attribute values, and the dlPFC modulates this computation by amplifying abstract/long-term attribute weights. Since the environment only provides 'palatability' on food resources, health is derived as an inverse proxy (health = 1 - palatability), creating the taste-health conflict central to the paradigm.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "CCV_a",
      "name": "composite_chosen_value",
      "description": "Weighted sum of taste and health attribute values for a candidate action/resource. The scalar value signal in vmPFC that is directly compared across options.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_h",
      "name": "health_weight",
      "description": "Current weight of the abstract/delayed (health) attribute in the vmPFC value computation. Modulated upward by dlPFC coupling.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.2,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_tau",
      "name": "taste_weight",
      "description": "Current weight of the immediate/hedonic (taste) attribute in the vmPFC value computation. Complement of health weight: w_tau = 1 - w_h.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.8,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "C",
      "name": "dlpfc_coupling",
      "description": "Degree of top-down modulation exerted by dlPFC on vmPFC at this decision step. Driven by conflict signal, goal activation, and remaining executive capacity.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "K",
      "name": "conflict_signal",
      "description": "Discrepancy between the tastiest and healthiest nearby resource options. Non-zero when the most palatable resource differs from the healthiest, quantified as the taste advantage of the temptation.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, "inf"]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "G",
      "name": "goal_activation",
      "description": "Strength of the active long-term health-goal representation. Decays over time, boosted when the agent successfully chooses the healthy option.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "D",
      "name": "depletion_level",
      "description": "Current depletion of dlPFC resources. 0 = fresh/undepled, 1 = exhausted. Increases when dlPFC coupling is high, passively recovers when coupling is low.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "P_a",
      "name": "action_probability",
      "description": "Probability of selecting each candidate action, computed via softmax over composite chosen values.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": null,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "w_h0",
      "name": "baseline_health_weight",
      "default": 0.2,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): vmPFC encodes primarily taste unless dlPFC is actively engaged"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_tau0",
      "name": "baseline_taste_weight",
      "default": 0.8,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): hedonic attribute dominates without top-down control"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma_C",
      "name": "coupling_gain_on_health_weight",
      "default": 0.6,
      "source": "Derived from Rangel (2013) P1: dlPFC amplifies abstract attribute weight; max w_h = 0.2 + 0.6 = 0.8"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "softmax_inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Standard value-based decision-making literature; validated in knowledge backbone (beta=5.0)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_D",
      "name": "depletion_rate_per_control_event",
      "default": 0.08,
      "source": "Approximation from cognitive depletion assumption; knowledge backbone depletion rate"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta_D",
      "name": "passive_recovery_rate",
      "default": 0.04,
      "source": "Knowledge backbone: recovery approximately half the depletion speed"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "lambda_K",
      "name": "conflict_sensitivity",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Derived from Rangel (2013) P4: conflict between systems triggers dlPFC engagement"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "eta_G",
      "name": "goal_decay_rate",
      "default": 0.05,
      "source": "Rangel (2013) assumption: goal activation decays without reinforcement"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "goal_boost",
      "name": "goal_boost_on_healthy_choice",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): successful self-control reinforces goal representation"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "distance_discount",
      "name": "distance_discount_factor",
      "default": 0.9,
      "source": "Standard temporal/spatial discounting for approach value in grid environments"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Compute conflict signal: measures disagreement between taste-best and health-best options among visible resources. If the tastiest resource is not the healthiest, K equals the taste advantage of the temptation over the health-optimal resource.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "taste_vals = {food_id: food.palatability for food in visible_foods}\nhealth_vals = {food_id: (1.0 - food.palatability) for food in visible_foods}\ntastiest_id = argmax(taste_vals)\nhealthiest_id = argmax(health_vals)\nif tastiest_id != healthiest_id:\n    K = taste_vals[tastiest_id] - taste_vals[healthiest_id]\nelse:\n    K = 0.0",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Compute dlPFC coupling strength: sigmoid of conflict signal, modulated by goal activation and remaining executive capacity (inverse of depletion).",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "sigmoid_K = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-lambda_K * K))\nC = sigmoid_K * G * (1.0 - D)",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Modulate attribute weights: health weight increases with dlPFC coupling from baseline; taste weight is the complement.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "w_h = w_h0 + gamma_C * C\nw_h = min(w_h, 1.0)\nw_tau = 1.0 - w_h",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Compute Composite Chosen Value for each candidate action. For 'eat' at a food location, CCV uses the food's attributes. For movement actions, CCV uses the best resource reachable one step in that direction, discounted by Manhattan distance. For 'stay' with no food, CCV = 0.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a in candidate_actions:\n    if a == 'eat' and food_at_position:\n        taste_a = food_at_position.palatability\n        health_a = 1.0 - food_at_position.palatability\n        CCV_a = w_tau * taste_a + w_h * health_a\n    elif a in ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right']:\n        target_pos = position + direction_delta(a)\n        nearest_food = nearest_food_from(target_pos, visible_foods)\n        if nearest_food is not None:\n            dist = manhattan_distance(target_pos, nearest_food)\n            taste_a = nearest_food.palatability\n            health_a = 1.0 - nearest_food.palatability\n            CCV_a = (w_tau * taste_a + w_h * health_a) * distance_discount ** dist\n        else:\n            CCV_a = 0.0\n    else:  # stay\n        CCV_a = 0.0",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Softmax action selection over composite chosen values.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a:\n    P_a = exp(beta * CCV_a) / sum(exp(beta * CCV_j) for j in candidate_actions)\nselected_action = sample from distribution P",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Update depletion level: increases proportionally to dlPFC coupling, decreases (recovery) proportionally to absence of coupling. Clamped to [0, 1].",
      "type": "discrete_update",
      "pseudocode": "D_new = D + alpha_D * C - beta_D * (1.0 - C)\nD = clamp(D_new, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Update goal activation: decays geometrically, boosted by 0.3 when the agent chose the healthier option on this step (ate the resource with lowest palatability, or moved toward it).",
      "type": "discrete_update",
      "pseudocode": "chose_healthy = (last_action == 'eat' and ate_food_palatability < 0.5)\nboost = goal_boost if chose_healthy else 0.0\nG_new = G * (1.0 - eta_G) + boost\nG = clamp(G_new, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Agent perceives its grid position and visible food resources (each having palatability). Health is derived as (1 - palatability). The agent computes conflict between taste-best and health-best options to trigger dlPFC engagement, modulates attribute weights, evaluates composite chosen values for all actions, then samples from a softmax distribution. After action execution, depletion and goal activation are updated.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# 1. Parse perception",
      "position = (perception.x, perception.y)",
      "visible_foods = perception.resources.food",
      "food_at_position = [f for f in visible_foods if f.x == position[0] and f.y == position[1]]",
      "",
      "# 2. Derive health attribute for each food (inverse of palatability)",
      "for each food in visible_foods:",
      "    food.health = 1.0 - food.palatability",
      "    food.taste = food.palatability",
      "",
      "# 3. Compute conflict K via R1",
      "K = compute_conflict(visible_foods)  # R1",
      "",
      "# 4. Compute dlPFC coupling C via R2",
      "C = sigmoid(lambda_K * K) * G * (1.0 - D)  # R2",
      "",
      "# 5. Compute attribute weights via R3",
      "w_h = w_h0 + gamma_C * C",
      "w_tau = 1.0 - w_h",
      "",
      "# 6. Compute CCV for each action via R4",
      "action_ccvs = {}",
      "candidate_actions = ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right','stay']",
      "if food_at_position:",
      "    candidate_actions.append('eat')",
      "for a in candidate_actions:",
      "    action_ccvs[a] = compute_ccv(a, position, visible_foods, w_tau, w_h)  # R4",
      "",
      "# 7. Softmax action selection via R5",
      "action = softmax_sample(action_ccvs, beta)  # R5",
      "",
      "# 8. Return chosen action",
      "return Action(action)"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception.x, perception.y)",
      "visible_foods": "perception.resources.food  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}",
      "food_at_position": "[f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y]",
      "taste_of_food": "food.palatability  # direct mapping",
      "health_of_food": "1.0 - food.palatability  # derived inverse proxy: low palatability = high health",
      "ate_food": "perception.last_action_result  # check if eat was successful",
      "grid_dims": "(perception.grid_width, perception.grid_height)"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 from env); agent internally values via CCV which weights taste vs health"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Conflict triggers dlPFC engagement: when high-palatability and low-palatability foods are both nearby, coupling C should increase",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent near foods with palatability 0.9 and 0.2 → assert C > 0.3"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Depletion accumulates under sustained conflict: after many conflict trials, D increases and C decreases",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 50 steps with continuous conflict → assert D > 0.5 and C at step 50 < C at step 1"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Without conflict, agent prefers high-palatability food (taste-dominated)",
      "test_pseudocode": "place only high-palatability foods → assert agent moves toward highest palatability"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "With active dlPFC (high G, low D, high conflict), agent shifts preference toward low-palatability (healthy) food",
      "test_pseudocode": "set G=1.0, D=0.0, place both food types → over 100 trials, assert fraction choosing low-palatability > 0.4"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Goal activation decays without healthy choices and recovers with healthy choices",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 30 steps eating only high-palatability → assert G < 0.2; then eat low-palatability 5 times → assert G > 0.5"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357"
  ]
}
