{
  "formulation_id": "algebraic-closed-form-ddm-with-softmax-action-selection",
  "paradigm": "drift-diffusion-model",
  "name": "Algebraic Closed-Form DDM with Softmax Action Selection",
  "description": "Uses the exact closed-form solutions for first-passage time statistics of the Wiener process (Bogacz et al., 2006) to analytically compute per-action choice probability and expected decision time, avoiding simulation of stochastic trajectories. Each action's drift rate is derived from perceptual proximity to resources. The DDM-predicted accuracy P_i and mean decision time T_bar_i are combined with a learned Q-value into a composite action value V_i. Actions are selected via softmax sampling over these composite values. This formulation implements postulates P2 (drift rate reflects stimulus quality) and P3 (speed-accuracy trade-off controlled by boundary separation) in an algebraically tractable way, making it computationally fast while preserving the theoretical grounding of the DDM. After each step, the chosen action's Q-value is updated via a simple temporal-difference rule.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "P_i",
      "name": "choice_probability",
      "description": "Closed-form probability that a DDM accumulator for action i would hit the upper boundary, given drift rate v_i, boundary a, and diffusion coefficient sigma. Derived from the Wiener first-passage solution.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "T_bar_i",
      "name": "expected_decision_time",
      "description": "Mean first-passage time for the accumulator for action i, derived from the Wiener process closed-form solution. Used as a time-cost penalty.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1000.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V_i",
      "name": "composite_action_value",
      "description": "Combined value of action i: accuracy-weighted benefit minus time cost plus learned utility. The decision criterion for action selection.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-100.0, 100.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "pi_i",
      "name": "action_selection_probability",
      "description": "Softmax probability of selecting action i, derived from composite values V_i and inverse temperature beta.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.1667,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "Q_i",
      "name": "learned_action_utility",
      "description": "Running estimate of expected reward from taking action i. Updated via temporal-difference learning after each step.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-5.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "v_i",
      "name": "drift_rate",
      "description": "Drift rate for action i computed from perceptual signal. Positive values indicate evidence favoring the action; higher magnitude means stronger evidence.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-5.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "s_i",
      "name": "perceptual_signal",
      "description": "Raw perceptual signal for action i derived from resource proximity or resource presence on cell.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "a",
      "name": "boundary_separation",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Ratcliff & McKoon (2008); typical value for moderate caution"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "z_rel",
      "name": "relative_starting_point",
      "default": 0.5,
      "source": "Unbiased default; Ratcliff & Rouder (1998)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma",
      "name": "diffusion_coefficient",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard scaling convention; Ratcliff (1978)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "softmax_inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Controls exploration-exploitation trade-off; knowledge backbone default from Run 542c7e41"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha",
      "name": "learning_rate",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard RL learning rate for Q-value updates"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "k_v",
      "name": "drift_rate_scaling",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Maps perceptual signal to drift rate units; linked to postulate P2"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "lambda_t",
      "name": "time_cost_penalty",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Penalizes slow decisions; implements speed-accuracy trade-off per postulate P3"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_P",
      "name": "accuracy_weight",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Relative importance of DDM-predicted accuracy in composite value"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_Q",
      "name": "utility_weight",
      "default": 0.5,
      "source": "Relative importance of learned reward signal in composite value"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "epsilon",
      "name": "zero_drift_guard",
      "default": 0.0001,
      "source": "Numerical guard for v_i near zero in Eq. 3 (L'Hopital limit); standard numerical practice"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Drift rate from perception: scales the perceptual signal by k_v to produce a drift rate for each action.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action i:\n  v_i = k_v * s_i",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Closed-form choice probability: exact DDM accuracy from Wiener first-passage analysis (Bogacz et al., 2006, Eq. 2.8). Assumes unbiased starting point z = a/2.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "P_i = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-2.0 * v_i * a / (sigma ** 2)))",
      "source_postulate": "P5"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Expected decision time: closed-form mean first-passage time (Bogacz et al., 2006, Eq. 2.10). Uses numerical guard for near-zero drift.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "if abs(v_i) < epsilon:\n  T_bar_i = (a ** 2) / (2.0 * sigma ** 2)\nelse:\n  T_bar_i = (a / (2.0 * v_i)) * tanh(v_i * a / (sigma ** 2))",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Composite action value: combines DDM-predicted accuracy (higher is better), a time-cost penalty (slower decisions are penalized), and a learned utility term.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "V_i = w_P * P_i - lambda_t * T_bar_i + w_Q * Q_i",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Softmax action selection: converts composite values into a probability distribution over actions using the Boltzmann softmax with inverse temperature beta.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "max_V = max(V_i for all i)  # numerical stability\npi_i = exp(beta * (V_i - max_V)) / sum(exp(beta * (V_j - max_V)) for all j)\nR = sample from categorical distribution with probabilities pi_i",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Q-value update: simple temporal-difference update for the chosen action only.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "Q_R = Q_R + alpha * (reward_observed - Q_R)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Per-step decision: compute perceptual signals, derive drift rates, evaluate closed-form DDM accuracy and decision time, form composite values, and sample action via softmax. No internal simulation loop needed — all computations are algebraic. After reward feedback, update the Q-value for the chosen action.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# Step 1: Parse perception",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_list = perception['resources'].get('food', [])",
      "food_on_cell = any(f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1] for f in food_list)",
      "grid_w, grid_h = perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height']",
      "",
      "# Step 2: Compute perceptual signals s_i for each action",
      "actions = ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right', 'stay', 'eat']",
      "direction_deltas = {'move_up': (0,-1), 'move_down': (0,1), 'move_left': (-1,0), 'move_right': (1,0)}",
      "for each action i:",
      "  if i in direction_deltas:",
      "    dx, dy = direction_deltas[i]",
      "    neighbor = (pos[0]+dx, pos[1]+dy)",
      "    d_i = min Manhattan distance from neighbor to any food (large number if no food)",
      "    s_i = 1.0 / (1.0 + d_i)",
      "  elif i == 'eat':",
      "    s_i = 1.0 if food_on_cell else -0.5",
      "  elif i == 'stay':",
      "    s_i = 0.0",
      "",
      "# Step 3: Compute drift rates via R1",
      "v_i = k_v * s_i  # for each action i",
      "",
      "# Step 4: Compute DDM accuracy P_i via R2",
      "P_i = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-2.0 * v_i * a / sigma**2))  # for each action i",
      "",
      "# Step 5: Compute expected decision time T_bar_i via R3",
      "for each action i:",
      "  if abs(v_i) < epsilon:",
      "    T_bar_i = a**2 / (2.0 * sigma**2)",
      "  else:",
      "    T_bar_i = (a / (2.0 * v_i)) * tanh(v_i * a / sigma**2)",
      "",
      "# Step 6: Compute composite value V_i via R4",
      "V_i = w_P * P_i - lambda_t * T_bar_i + w_Q * Q_i  # for each action i",
      "",
      "# Step 7: Softmax selection via R5",
      "max_V = max(V_i for all i)",
      "pi_i = exp(beta * (V_i - max_V)) / sum(exp(beta * (V_j - max_V)) for all j)",
      "chosen = sample_categorical(actions, probabilities=pi_i)",
      "",
      "# Step 8: Return chosen action",
      "return Action(chosen)",
      "",
      "# Step 9 (in update method): Update Q_chosen via R6",
      "Q[chosen] = Q[chosen] + alpha * (reward - Q[chosen])"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food']",
      "food_on_cell": "any(f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y for f in perception['resources']['food'])",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "step_number": "perception['step']"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect with reward 1.0; move/stay actions yield reward 0.0"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Agent moves toward food: actions pointing toward nearby food get higher drift rates, higher P_i, and thus higher V_i, making them more likely under softmax.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place food at (5,5), agent at (0,0) → over 50 steps, assert agent's distance to food decreases on average"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Agent eats when on food: the eat action gets s_eat = 1.0 when food is on cell, producing high drift and high P_eat, dominating the softmax.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent on food cell → run decide() 100 times → assert eat is chosen > 70% of the time"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Speed-accuracy trade-off: increasing boundary separation 'a' makes P_i closer to 1.0 but increases T_bar_i, shifting the balance in V_i. Higher lambda_t favors faster (lower-confidence) actions.",
      "test_pseudocode": "compare action distributions with lambda_t=0.01 vs lambda_t=1.0 → higher lambda favors actions with lower T_bar"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Q-learning adapts over time: initially Q_i = 0 for all actions, but after repeated eat rewards, Q_eat increases, further biasing selection toward eat when on food.",
      "test_pseudocode": "simulate 50 eat-reward cycles → assert Q['eat'] > 0.5"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "No internal simulation loop: decision is computed in O(n_actions) algebraic operations, not iterative accumulation. Faster than Formulation 1.",
      "test_pseudocode": "time decide() → assert < 1ms per call"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Ratcliff, R. (1978). A theory of memory retrieval. Psychological Review, 85(2), 59-108. DOI: 10.1037/0033-295X.85.2.59",
    "Ratcliff, R., & McKoon, G. (2008). The diffusion decision model: Theory and data for two-choice decision tasks. Neural Computation, 20(4), 873-922. DOI: 10.1162/neco.2008.12-06-420",
    "Ratcliff, R., & Rouder, J. N. (1998). Modeling response times for two-choice decisions. Psychological Science, 9(5), 347-356. DOI: 10.1111/1467-9280.00067",
    "Bogacz, R., Brown, E., Moehlis, J., Holmes, P., & Cohen, J. D. (2006). The physics of optimal decision making. Psychological Review, 113(4), 700-765. DOI: 10.1037/0033-295X.113.4.700"
  ]
}

metadata.output: Written 12675 chars to reasoner/drift-diffusion-model/algebraic-closed-form-ddm-with-softmax-action-selection.json

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "hormonal-modulation-with-softmax-reinforcement-learning",
  "paradigm": "homeostatic-regulation-of-food-valuation",
  "name": "Hormonal Modulation with Softmax Reinforcement Learning",
  "description": "A model-free Q-learning agent whose action selection is modulated by simulated ghrelin (orexigenic) and leptin (anorexigenic) hormone proxies. Ghrelin rises with fasting and drops after eating; leptin tracks energy stores. A composite hormonal modulator M multiplicatively scales food-related Q-values before softmax action selection, so the same learned values produce different behavior under hunger vs. satiety. The reward signal is also modulated by M, causing the agent to learn higher values for food when it was hungry at the time of eating. Grounded in Rangel (2013) hormonal modulation of decision circuitry (Postulates P2, P4), Jacquier (2016) ghrelin/leptin dynamics, and the wanting/liking distinction referenced in Rangel (2013).",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "E",
      "name": "energy_store",
      "description": "Normalized internal energy reserve. Depleted by basal metabolism, replenished by eating.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "G",
      "name": "ghrelin_proxy",
      "description": "Short-term orexigenic hunger signal. Rises linearly each step during fasting, drops sharply after eating. Proxies plasma ghrelin dynamics.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.3,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "L",
      "name": "leptin_proxy",
      "description": "Long-term anorexigenic signal proportional to energy stores (proxy for fat-mass-derived leptin). High when energy stores are full, low when depleted.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "M",
      "name": "hormonal_modulator",
      "description": "Composite gain factor applied to food-related Q-values. M > 1 when ghrelin is high and leptin is low (hungry), M < 1 when leptin dominates (satiated). Captures bidirectional hormonal modulation of food value.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 1.0,
      "range": [0.33, 3.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "Q",
      "name": "action_value_table",
      "description": "Learned Q-values for state-action pairs. State is discretized as (agent_x, agent_y, food_at_cell, nearest_food_direction, hunger_bin). Updated via TD-learning.",
      "type": "dict",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "delta",
      "name": "td_error",
      "description": "Temporal difference error: discrepancy between received reward plus discounted future value and current Q-value. Drives Q-table updates.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "R",
      "name": "reward_signal",
      "description": "Immediate reward signal. Modulated by hormonal state M when eating (hungry eating is more rewarding), fixed small negative cost for movement steps.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "ate",
      "name": "ate_flag",
      "description": "Binary indicator: 1 if the agent successfully consumed food on the current step, 0 otherwise.",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": 0,
      "range": [0, 1]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_E",
      "name": "basal_metabolic_cost",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Jacquier (2016), adapted to grid timescale"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_food",
      "name": "energy_gain_from_eating",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Normalized; represents substantial meal (Jacquier 2016 energy intake scaling)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "lambda_G",
      "name": "ghrelin_rise_rate",
      "default": 0.03,
      "source": "Jacquier (2016): ghrelin rises pre-prandially during fasting"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "kappa_G",
      "name": "ghrelin_suppression_on_eating",
      "default": 0.5,
      "source": "Jacquier (2016): rapid post-prandial ghrelin suppression (~50% drop)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "k_L",
      "name": "leptin_coupling_exponent",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Jacquier (2016): leptin proportional to fat mass; exponent 1.0 = linear coupling"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_G",
      "name": "ghrelin_weight_in_modulator",
      "default": 2.0,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): ghrelin amplifies food value signals in vmPFC/VTA"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_L",
      "name": "leptin_weight_in_modulator",
      "default": 2.0,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): leptin suppresses food value signals"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha",
      "name": "learning_rate",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard Q-learning rate (Sutton & Barto)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma",
      "name": "discount_factor",
      "default": 0.9,
      "source": "Standard RL temporal discounting"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Moderate exploration-exploitation balance for softmax"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_food",
      "name": "base_food_reward",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Rangel (2013) normalized value scale"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_step",
      "name": "step_cost",
      "default": -0.02,
      "source": "Small movement penalty to discourage aimless wandering"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Energy dynamics: energy decreases by basal metabolic cost each step and increases when food is consumed.",
      "type": "difference_equation",
      "pseudocode": "E = clamp(E - alpha_E + c_food * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Ghrelin dynamics: ghrelin rises linearly each step during fasting and is sharply suppressed post-prandially, modeling the short-term orexigenic signal.",
      "type": "difference_equation",
      "pseudocode": "G = clamp(G + lambda_G - kappa_G * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Leptin proxy: leptin is a power function of current energy stores, modeling the proportionality of leptin to fat mass.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "L = E ** k_L",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Hormonal modulator: composite gain factor that amplifies food Q-values when ghrelin is high (hungry) and suppresses them when leptin is high (satiated). This is the core mechanism by which homeostatic state enters the decision circuitry.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "M = (1.0 + w_G * G) / (1.0 + w_L * L)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "State-dependent reward: eating reward is modulated by hormonal state M, so eating while hungry is more rewarding than eating while satiated. Non-eating actions incur a small step cost.",
      "type": "conditional",
      "pseudocode": "if ate == 1:\n    R = M * r_food\nelse:\n    R = c_step",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "TD-error and Q-value update: standard Q-learning update using the state-dependent reward signal.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "delta = R + gamma * max(Q[s_next, a'] for a' in actions) - Q[s_current, a_current]\nQ[s_current, a_current] = Q[s_current, a_current] + alpha * delta",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Modulated softmax action selection: Q-values for food-related actions (eat, move toward food) are multiplied by M before softmax. Non-food actions (stay) use unmodulated Q-values. This implements the hormonal gating of food valuation at decision time.",
      "type": "action_selection",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a in available_actions:\n    if a == 'eat' or a is a move toward food:\n        Q_tilde[a] = M * Q[s, a]\n    else:\n        Q_tilde[a] = Q[s, a]\nP(a) = exp(beta * Q_tilde[a]) / sum(exp(beta * Q_tilde[j]) for j in available_actions)\nsample action from P",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Stochastic softmax action selection over hormonally modulated Q-values. The agent maintains a Q-table indexed by a discretized state encoding (position, local food presence, hunger level bin). Ghrelin and leptin proxies create a multiplicative hormonal modulator M that scales food-related Q-values before the softmax, causing the agent to strongly prefer eating when hungry and to be indifferent to food when satiated — even if the raw Q-values are the same. This implements Rangel (2013) Postulate P2: hormones modulate decision circuitry rather than bypassing it.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# 1. Determine eating outcome from last step",
      "ate = 1 if last_action_result indicates successful consumption else 0",
      "",
      "# 2. Update internal physiological state",
      "E = clamp(E - alpha_E + c_food * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "G = clamp(G + lambda_G - kappa_G * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "L = E ** k_L",
      "M = (1.0 + w_G * G) / (1.0 + w_L * L)",
      "",
      "# 3. Compute reward for the previous action (for Q-update)",
      "if ate == 1:",
      "    R = M * r_food",
      "else:",
      "    R = c_step",
      "",
      "# 4. Discretize state for Q-table lookup",
      "# State encoding: (x, y, food_at_cell, nearest_food_dx_sign, nearest_food_dy_sign, hunger_bin)",
      "# hunger_bin: 0 if G < 0.33, 1 if 0.33 <= G < 0.67, 2 if G >= 0.67",
      "food_at_cell = any(f for f in food_list if f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y)",
      "nearest = nearest_food(x, y, food_list)",
      "dx_sign = sign(nearest.x - x)  # -1, 0, or 1",
      "dy_sign = sign(nearest.y - y)  # -1, 0, or 1",
      "hunger_bin = 0 if G < 0.33 else (1 if G < 0.67 else 2)",
      "s = (x, y, food_at_cell, dx_sign, dy_sign, hunger_bin)",
      "",
      "# 5. Q-learning update for the PREVIOUS step's (s, a) pair",
      "if previous_state is not None:",
      "    delta = R + gamma * max(Q[s, a_prime] for a_prime in actions) - Q[prev_s, prev_a]",
      "    Q[prev_s, prev_a] += alpha * delta",
      "",
      "# 6. Compute modulated Q-values for action selection",
      "# Determine which actions are 'food-related'",
      "# eat is food-related; moves that decrease distance to nearest food are food-related",
      "for a in available_actions:",
      "    if a == 'eat' or move_decreases_food_distance(a, x, y, nearest):",
      "        Q_tilde = M * Q[s, a]",
      "    else:",
      "        Q_tilde = Q[s, a]",
      "    modulated_values[a] = Q_tilde",
      "",
      "# 7. Softmax action selection",
      "max_q = max(modulated_values.values())  # for numerical stability",
      "probs = {a: exp(beta * (q - max_q)) for a, q in modulated_values.items()}",
      "total = sum(probs.values())",
      "probs = {a: p / total for a, p in probs.items()}",
      "selected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=probs)",
      "",
      "# 8. Store current state and action for next step's Q-update",
      "prev_s = s",
      "prev_a = selected_action",
      "return Action(selected_action)"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food']  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}",
      "ate_flag": "1 if perception['last_action_result'] indicates eat success (resource consumed) else 0",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "food_at_cell": "any(f for f in perception['resources']['food'] if f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y'])",
      "step": "perception['step']"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect with env reward 1.0 (used as signal for ate_flag). Internal reward R is computed by R5 using hormonal modulator M, not the raw env reward."
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Ghrelin rises during fasting: without eating, G increases linearly toward 1.0, amplifying food value.",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 30 steps without food → assert G increases by ~lambda_G per step"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Ghrelin drops after eating: successful eat action causes sharp ghrelin suppression.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set G = 0.8 then eat → assert G drops by kappa_G to ~0.3"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Hormonal modulator amplifies food seeking when hungry: M >> 1 when G is high and E is low.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set G = 0.9, E = 0.1 → compute M → assert M > 2.0"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Hormonal modulator suppresses food seeking when satiated: M ≈ 1 or M < 1 when G is low and E is high.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set G = 0.1, E = 0.9 → compute M → assert M < 1.0"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Agent learns to approach food: after sufficient training, Q-values for actions that move toward food should be higher than those moving away.",
      "test_pseudocode": "train for 500 steps → for states with food nearby, assert Q[move_toward] > Q[move_away]"
    },
    {
      "id": "B6",
      "description": "State-dependent eating: same spatial state produces different action probabilities under high vs low ghrelin due to M modulation.",
      "test_pseudocode": "fix Q-values, set M = 2.5 → compute P(eat); set M = 0.5 → compute P(eat); assert P_hungry(eat) >> P_satiated(eat)"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Jacquier, M. (2016). Mathematical modeling of the hormonal regulation of food intake and body weight. Doctoral thesis, Université Claude Bernard Lyon 1. HAL: tel-01273347",
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357"
  ]
}

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "incentive-salience-dual-process-agent-wantingliking-dissociation",
  "paradigm": "pavlovian-control-of-food-approach",
  "name": "Incentive Salience Dual-Process Agent (Wanting–Liking Dissociation)",
  "description": "A dual-channel Pavlovian agent maintaining separate 'wanting' (dopamine-mediated incentive salience) and 'liking' (opioid-mediated hedonic value) maps, each with independent learning dynamics. Wanting drives approach vigor proportional to hunger, while liking tracks hedonic experience modulated by alliesthesia (food pleasantness depends on hunger state). The two channels are combined via weighted summation for softmax action selection. This architecture captures the empirical dissociation between wanting and liking (e.g., high wanting with low liking in addiction-like states, or suppressed wanting in satiety despite unchanged liking). Different learning rates (fast wanting, slow liking) match neuropharmacological data on dopamine vs. opioid plasticity.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "W",
      "name": "wanting_values",
      "description": "Dictionary mapping grid cell (x,y) to incentive salience (wanting) value. Represents dopamine-mediated motivational attribution to each location as a conditioned stimulus.",
      "type": "dict[tuple[int,int], float]",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "L",
      "name": "liking_values",
      "description": "Dictionary mapping grid cell (x,y) to hedonic (liking) value. Represents opioid-mediated pleasantness expectation for each location.",
      "type": "dict[tuple[int,int], float]",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "delta_W",
      "name": "wanting_prediction_error",
      "description": "Reward prediction error driving the wanting channel update. Reflects dopaminergic phasic signal.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-2.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "delta_L",
      "name": "liking_prediction_error",
      "description": "Hedonic surprise driving the liking channel update. Reflects opioid-mediated hedonic mismatch. Target is hunger-modulated (alliesthesia).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-2.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "h_t",
      "name": "hunger_level",
      "description": "Internal deprivation state normalized to [0,1]. Modulates wanting multiplicatively and liking via alliesthesia power law.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_t",
      "name": "reward_received",
      "description": "Scalar food reward obtained at time t. 0 if no food eaten, r_food if food consumed.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "Q_total",
      "name": "combined_action_values",
      "description": "Weighted sum of wanting and liking contributions for each action. Recomputed each step.",
      "type": "dict[str, float]",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-Infinity, Infinity]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "P_a",
      "name": "action_probabilities",
      "description": "Softmax probability distribution over available actions. Recomputed each step from Q_total.",
      "type": "dict[str, float]",
      "initial_value": null,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_W",
      "name": "wanting_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.12,
      "source": "Faster plasticity for dopamine-mediated learning; validated in prior pipeline run (confidence 0.58)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_L",
      "name": "liking_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.05,
      "source": "Slower adaptation for opioid-mediated hedonic signal; validated in prior pipeline run (confidence 0.58)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Standard softmax parameter; validated in prior pipeline runs"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu",
      "name": "hunger_wanting_gain",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Multiplicative gating of wanting by hunger; Berridge & Robinson (2003) as cited in Rangel (2013); validated in prior pipeline run (confidence 0.57)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "lam",
      "name": "alliesthesia_coefficient",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Power-law exponent for hunger modulation of liking (pleasantness depends on deprivation); Rangel (2013)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_W",
      "name": "wanting_weight",
      "default": 0.6,
      "source": "Relative contribution of wanting to combined value; wanting dominates approach behavior per incentive salience theory"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_L",
      "name": "liking_weight",
      "default": 0.4,
      "source": "Relative contribution of liking; w_W + w_L = 1.0"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_food",
      "name": "food_reward_magnitude",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Matches env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_h",
      "name": "hunger_increment_rate",
      "default": 0.02,
      "source": "Per-step hunger growth reflecting metabolic demand"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma_sat",
      "name": "satiation_decrement",
      "default": 0.20,
      "source": "Hunger reduction upon eating; ~5 meals for full satiation"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_step",
      "name": "movement_cost",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Small energetic cost of locomotion"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "W_max",
      "name": "max_wanting",
      "default": 2.0,
      "source": "Upper bound for wanting values; 2x max single-step reward"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "L_max",
      "name": "max_liking",
      "default": 2.0,
      "source": "Upper bound for liking values; 2x max single-step reward"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Wanting prediction error: difference between received reward and current wanting value of the occupied cell. Reflects dopaminergic RPE.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "delta_W = r_t - W.get((x, y), 0.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Wanting value update: W(s) is incremented by alpha_W times the wanting prediction error, clipped to [0, W_max].",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "W[(x, y)] = clip(W.get((x, y), 0.0) + alpha_W * delta_W, 0.0, W_max)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Liking prediction error (hedonic surprise): target is reward modulated by hunger via alliesthesia power law h^lambda. Food is more pleasant when hungry.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "delta_L = r_t * (h_t ** lam) - L.get((x, y), 0.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Liking value update: L(s) is incremented by alpha_L times the liking prediction error, clipped to [0, L_max]. Slower learning than wanting.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "L[(x, y)] = clip(L.get((x, y), 0.0) + alpha_L * delta_L, 0.0, L_max)",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Hunger dynamics: hunger increases by alpha_h each step and decreases by gamma_sat upon eating, clipped to [0, 1].",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "ate = 1.0 if (action == 'eat' and eat_succeeded) else 0.0\nh_t = clip(h_t + alpha_h - gamma_sat * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Hunger-modulated wanting contribution to action value: wanting drives approach proportional to hunger × learned wanting of destination state.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "Q_W = mu * h_t * W.get(dest, 0.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Liking contribution to action value: hedonic value of destination state modulated by alliesthesia (hunger power law).",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "Q_L = (h_t ** lam) * L.get(dest, 0.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R8",
      "description": "Combined Pavlovian action value: weighted sum of wanting and liking contributions minus movement cost.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "Q_total[a] = w_W * Q_W + w_L * Q_L - c_step * (1 if a is movement else 0)",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R9",
      "description": "Softmax action selection: action probabilities proportional to exp(beta * Q_total(a)).",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "max_q = max(Q_total.values())\nfor a in actions:\n  P_a[a] = exp(beta * (Q_total[a] - max_q))\nZ = sum(P_a.values())\nfor a in actions:\n  P_a[a] = P_a[a] / Z\nselected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=P_a)",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Dual-process Pavlovian agent: perceive environment, compute wanting and liking contributions for each action's destination state, combine via weighted sum, select via softmax. On update, maintain separate wanting (fast, dopaminergic) and liking (slow, opioidergic) learning channels. The wanting channel is gated by raw hunger; the liking channel is gated by alliesthesia (hunger power law). This produces dissociable approach patterns: high hunger amplifies wanting-driven approach even when liking is low, while satiety suppresses wanting but leaves liking relatively stable.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# 1. PERCEIVE",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_list = perception['resources'].get('food', [])",
      "food_positions = set((f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list)",
      "food_at_position = pos in food_positions",
      "grid_w, grid_h = perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height']",
      "",
      "# 2. COMPUTE DESTINATION CELLS",
      "destinations = {",
      "  'move_up':    (pos[0], pos[1] - 1),",
      "  'move_down':  (pos[0], pos[1] + 1),",
      "  'move_left':  (pos[0] - 1, pos[1]),",
      "  'move_right': (pos[0] + 1, pos[1]),",
      "  'stay':       pos,",
      "  'eat':        pos",
      "}",
      "",
      "# 3. COMPUTE Q_total(a) for each action (R6, R7, R8)",
      "Q_total = {}",
      "for action_name, dest in destinations.items():",
      "  dx, dy = dest",
      "  # Check action availability",
      "  if action_name == 'eat' and not food_at_position:",
      "    Q_total['eat'] = -1e9",
      "    continue",
      "  if action_name in ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right']:",
      "    if not (0 <= dx < grid_w and 0 <= dy < grid_h):",
      "      Q_total[action_name] = -1e9",
      "      continue",
      "  # Wanting contribution",
      "  Q_W = mu * h_t * W.get(dest, 0.0)",
      "  # Liking contribution",
      "  Q_L = (h_t ** lam) * L.get(dest, 0.0)",
      "  # Combined value",
      "  is_movement = action_name in ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right']",
      "  Q_total[action_name] = w_W * Q_W + w_L * Q_L - c_step * (1.0 if is_movement else 0.0)",
      "",
      "# 4. SOFTMAX ACTION SELECTION (R9)",
      "max_q = max(Q_total.values())",
      "exp_vals = {a: exp(beta * (q - max_q)) for a, q in Q_total.items()}",
      "Z = sum(exp_vals.values())",
      "P_a = {a: v / Z for a, v in exp_vals.items()}",
      "selected_action = random_choice(list(P_a.keys()), weights=list(P_a.values()))",
      "",
      "# 5. RETURN action",
      "return Action(selected_action)"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food'] — list of dicts with 'x', 'y', 'type', 'palatability'",
      "food_at_position": "any(f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y'] for f in perception['resources']['food'])",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "ate_food": "last_action_result — check if eat action succeeded (consumed == true)",
      "reward_received": "reward argument in update() method; 1.0 from ConsumeEffect on successful eat",
      "palatability": "food_list[i]['palatability'] — available but not currently used; could modulate r_t in extended version"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions yield reward = 0.0"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Wanting–liking dissociation: after extensive training, W(s) converges faster than L(s) due to alpha_W > alpha_L",
      "test_pseudocode": "train agent at food cell for 20 steps → assert W[(x,y)] > L[(x,y)]"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Hunger suppresses approach via wanting channel: in sated state, approach probability decreases even with high W(s)",
      "test_pseudocode": "set W[(5,5)]=1.5, h_t=0.05 → assert P(approach (5,5)) is low; set h_t=0.9 → assert P(approach (5,5)) is high"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Alliesthesia modulates liking: food consumed when hungry generates higher liking PE than when sated",
      "test_pseudocode": "set h_t=0.9, eat → delta_L = 1.0 * 0.9^1.0 - L ≈ 0.9; set h_t=0.1, eat → delta_L = 1.0 * 0.1^1.0 - L ≈ 0.1"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Dual extinction: without food, both W and L decay toward 0, but at different rates",
      "test_pseudocode": "train W and L at (5,5), remove food, visit 30 times → assert both decrease; W decreases faster than L"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Combined channel produces approach: even if wanting is low, high liking can still drive approach (and vice versa)",
      "test_pseudocode": "set W[(3,3)]=0.0, L[(3,3)]=1.5, h_t=0.5 → assert some approach to (3,3) occurs via liking channel"
    },
    {
      "id": "B6",
      "description": "Hunger dynamics: hunger increases without eating, decreases with eating",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 50 steps without food → assert h_t increases; eat 3 times → assert h_t decreases by ~0.6"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357",
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Berridge, K. C., & Robinson, T. E. (2003). Parsing reward. As cited in Rangel (2013) for wanting–liking dissociation and hunger–wanting coupling parameter μ."
  ]
}

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "attribute-based-evidence-accumulation-drift-diffusion",
  "paradigm": "attribute-based-value-computation",
  "name": "Attribute-Based Evidence Accumulation (Drift-Diffusion)",
  "description": "Dynamic stochastic decision process where value evidence for each candidate action accumulates over internal deliberation time steps. Drift rates are determined by attribute-weighted option values (immediate proximity attribute + abstract palatability attribute), and noise is scaled by attribute conflict (disagreement between attribute dimensions). Actions are selected when an accumulator first crosses a decision threshold, or by highest accumulated evidence at timeout. This produces naturalistic choice stochasticity and captures the prediction from Rangel (2013) that attribute conflict leads to slower, noisier decisions. Attribute weights are modulated by hunger and updated via reward prediction error. Grounded in postulates P1 (weighted summation), P3 (weight modulation), and Rangel (2013) prediction on conflict and reaction times.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "E_a",
      "name": "evidence_accumulator",
      "description": "Running total of value evidence favoring action a at deliberation step t. One accumulator per candidate action. Reset to 0 at the start of each simulation step's deliberation.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu_a",
      "name": "drift_rate",
      "description": "Mean rate of evidence accumulation for action a, derived from the attribute-weighted value of the option targeted by that action.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "a_imm",
      "name": "immediate_attribute_value",
      "description": "Proximity-based desirability of the resource targeted by the action. For eat: 1.0 if food at position. For movement: 1.0 - manhattan_distance / max_distance to nearest food from next cell. For stay: 0.0.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "a_abs",
      "name": "abstract_attribute_value",
      "description": "Quality/palatability attribute of the targeted resource. Derived from food palatability: a_abs = 2 * (palatability - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1. Range [-1, 1].",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_imm",
      "name": "immediate_weight",
      "description": "Weight for immediate attribute, dynamically set by hunger state. Computed via functional form ensuring w_imm + w_abs = 1.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_abs",
      "name": "abstract_weight",
      "description": "Weight for abstract attribute. Complement of w_imm.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "H_t",
      "name": "hunger",
      "description": "Internal hunger drive. Rises by eta each step, drops by R_food when food is consumed. 0=sated, 1=maximally hungry.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma_a",
      "name": "noise_magnitude",
      "description": "Standard deviation of Gaussian noise in the evidence accumulation for action a, scaled by base noise and attribute conflict.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.3,
      "range": [0.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "conflict_a",
      "name": "attribute_conflict",
      "description": "Degree of disagreement between immediate and abstract attribute values for the option targeted by action a. Higher conflict -> more noise -> more stochastic and slower decisions.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "theta",
      "name": "decision_threshold",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Standard DDM calibration; agent commits when E_a >= theta"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma_0",
      "name": "base_noise",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Typical DDM noise level for moderate stochasticity"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "kappa",
      "name": "conflict_noise_scaling",
      "default": 0.5,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): attribute conflict increases RT and noise"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "T_max",
      "name": "max_deliberation_steps",
      "default": 10,
      "source": "Simulation constraint enforcing bounded rationality"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta_w",
      "name": "weight_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.05,
      "source": "Standard reinforcement learning rate for attribute weight updates"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "eta",
      "name": "hunger_rise_rate",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Simulation convention (slow metabolic timescale)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "R_food",
      "name": "hunger_restoration_from_eating",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Simulation convention"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Compute attribute weights as a function of hunger. Hunger monotonically increases the immediate-attribute weight. No attentional learning in this formulation — weights are purely state-driven.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "w_imm = (1 + H_t) / (2 + H_t)\nw_abs = 1.0 / (2 + H_t)\n# w_imm + w_abs = 1 always",
      "source_postulate": "P3, P5"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Compute attribute values for each candidate action, mapping food proximity to immediate attribute and food palatability to abstract attribute.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "max_dist = grid_width + grid_height - 2\nfor each action a in available_actions:\n  if a == 'eat':\n    if food_at_agent_position:\n      a_imm = 1.0\n      a_abs = 2.0 * (food.palatability - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0\n    else:\n      a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0\n  elif a == 'stay':\n    a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0\n  else:  # movement action\n    next_pos = position + direction_delta(a)\n    nearest = find_nearest_food(next_pos, food_list)\n    if nearest:\n      dist = manhattan_distance(next_pos, nearest)\n      a_imm = 1.0 - dist / max_dist\n      a_abs = 2.0 * (nearest.palatability - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0\n    else:\n      a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Compute drift rate for each action as the weighted linear sum of its attribute values.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "mu_a = w_imm * a_imm_a + w_abs * a_abs_a",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Compute attribute conflict as absolute difference between immediate and abstract attribute values for the option targeted by each action.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "conflict_a = abs(a_imm_a - a_abs_a)",
      "source_postulate": "Rangel (2013) prediction: conflict -> slower, noisier choices"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Compute noise magnitude for each action, scaled by base noise and conflict.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "sigma_a = sigma_0 * (1 + kappa * conflict_a)",
      "source_postulate": "Rangel (2013): conflict increases decision noise and reaction time"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Evidence accumulation loop (discrete-time DDM). Each action has an independent accumulator starting at 0. At each deliberation step, drift + Gaussian noise is added. First accumulator to cross threshold wins.",
      "type": "stochastic_process",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a: E_a = 0.0\nfor t in range(1, T_max + 1):\n  for each action a:\n    xi = random_normal(0, 1)\n    E_a = E_a + mu_a + sigma_a * xi\n    if E_a >= theta:\n      return action a  # first to threshold\n# fallback if no threshold crossed\nreturn action with max E_a",
      "source_postulate": "P2, Rangel (2013) prediction on conflict-RT relationship"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Attribute weight update via reward prediction error after action execution. Weights shift toward attributes that correlate with positive surprise, then renormalized.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "RPE = reward - mu_selected\nw_imm = w_imm + beta_w * RPE * a_imm_selected\nw_abs = w_abs + beta_w * RPE * a_abs_selected\nw_imm = max(0.05, w_imm)\nw_abs = max(0.05, w_abs)\ntotal = w_imm + w_abs\nw_imm = w_imm / total\nw_abs = w_abs / total",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R8",
      "description": "Hunger state update. Rises each step, drops when food is consumed.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "ate = 1 if (action == 'eat' and consumed) else 0\nH_t = clip(H_t + eta - R_food * ate, 0, 1)",
      "source_postulate": "P5"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Each simulation step: (1) Read perception for position, food locations, hunger. (2) Compute hunger-driven attribute weights via R1. (3) For each of 6 actions, compute attribute values (R2), drift rates (R3), conflict (R4), and noise (R5). (4) Run evidence accumulation race (R6) over up to T_max deliberation steps. (5) Return the winning action. After execution: (6) Update hunger (R8). (7) Update attribute weights via RPE (R7). Key difference from Formulation 1: action selection is via competitive stochastic accumulation, not softmax. Attribute conflict explicitly increases noise and decision stochasticity.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# --- DECIDE PHASE ---",
      "food_list = perception['resources']['food']",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "grid_w = perception['grid_width']",
      "grid_h = perception['grid_height']",
      "max_dist = grid_w + grid_h - 2",
      "",
      "# R1: Attribute weights from hunger",
      "w_imm = (1 + H_t) / (2 + H_t)",
      "w_abs = 1.0 / (2 + H_t)",
      "",
      "# R2: Compute attribute values for each action",
      "actions = ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right', 'stay', 'eat']",
      "deltas = {'move_up': (0,-1), 'move_down': (0,1), 'move_left': (-1,0), 'move_right': (1,0)}",
      "food_at_pos = [f for f in food_list if f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1]]",
      "",
      "attr_values = {}  # action -> (a_imm, a_abs)",
      "for a in actions:",
      "  if a == 'eat':",
      "    if food_at_pos:",
      "      a_imm = 1.0",
      "      a_abs = 2.0 * (food_at_pos[0]['palatability'] - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0",
      "    else:",
      "      a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0",
      "  elif a == 'stay':",
      "    a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0",
      "  else:",
      "    dx, dy = deltas[a]",
      "    nx = clip(pos[0]+dx, 0, grid_w-1)",
      "    ny = clip(pos[1]+dy, 0, grid_h-1)",
      "    nearest = find_nearest_food((nx, ny), food_list)",
      "    if nearest:",
      "      dist = abs(nx-nearest['x']) + abs(ny-nearest['y'])",
      "      a_imm = 1.0 - dist / max_dist",
      "      a_abs = 2.0 * (nearest['palatability'] - 0.1) / 0.9 - 1.0",
      "    else:",
      "      a_imm = 0.0; a_abs = 0.0",
      "  attr_values[a] = (a_imm, a_abs)",
      "",
      "# R3: Drift rates",
      "drift = {a: w_imm * av[0] + w_abs * av[1] for a, av in attr_values.items()}",
      "",
      "# R4 + R5: Conflict and noise",
      "conflict = {a: abs(av[0] - av[1]) for a, av in attr_values.items()}",
      "noise = {a: sigma_0 * (1 + kappa * conflict[a]) for a in actions}",
      "",
      "# R6: Evidence accumulation race",
      "E = {a: 0.0 for a in actions}",
      "selected = None",
      "for t in range(1, T_max + 1):",
      "  for a in actions:",
      "    xi = random.gauss(0, 1)",
      "    E[a] = E[a] + drift[a] + noise[a] * xi",
      "    if E[a] >= theta and selected is None:",
      "      selected = a",
      "  if selected is not None:",
      "    break",
      "if selected is None:",
      "  selected = max(E, key=E.get)",
      "",
      "store drift[selected], attr_values[selected] for update phase",
      "return Action(selected)",
      "",
      "# --- UPDATE PHASE ---",
      "# R8: Hunger update",
      "ate = 1 if (selected == 'eat' and reward > 0) else 0",
      "H_t = clip(H_t + eta - R_food * ate, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "",
      "# R7: Weight update via RPE",
      "RPE = reward - drift[selected]",
      "a_imm_sel, a_abs_sel = attr_values[selected]",
      "w_imm = w_imm + beta_w * RPE * a_imm_sel",
      "w_abs = w_abs + beta_w * RPE * a_abs_sel",
      "w_imm = max(0.05, w_imm)",
      "w_abs = max(0.05, w_abs)",
      "total = w_imm + w_abs",
      "w_imm = w_imm / total",
      "w_abs = w_abs / total"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food']  # list of {'x': int, 'y': int, 'type': 'food', 'palatability': float}",
      "grid_dims": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "ate_food": "last_action_result.get('consumed', False) or (reward > 0 and action == 'eat')",
      "food_palatability": "resource['palatability'] for each resource in food_sources"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action -> ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 on successful consumption)"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "When hungry, agent biases toward closer food (higher w_imm -> higher drift for actions leading to nearby food).",
      "test_pseudocode": "set H_t = 0.9, two foods: near(dist=1) and far(dist=5, higher palatability) -> assert agent selects move-toward-near more often over 100 trials"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "High attribute conflict produces more variable (stochastic) choices. When a_imm and a_abs disagree strongly, noise is amplified.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set food with palatability=0.1 (low a_abs, high a_imm if close) -> measure choice entropy over 100 trials -> assert higher entropy than food with palatability=0.9"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Agent eats food when on the same cell, as eat action gets maximal drift rate (a_imm=1.0, positive a_abs).",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent on food with palatability=0.5, H_t=0.5 -> assert eat action selected > 50% of trials over 100 runs"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "With zero conflict (a_imm ~ a_abs), decisions are faster (threshold reached in fewer deliberation steps) and more deterministic.",
      "test_pseudocode": "record number of deliberation steps to threshold across many decisions -> assert mean steps lower when conflict < 0.2 vs conflict > 1.0"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Hunger rises steadily without eating, causing progressive shift to proximity-seeking behavior.",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 100 steps without food -> assert H_t > 0.95 and w_imm > 0.7"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717-1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9(7), 545-556. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357"
  ]
}

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "executive-resource-ode-with-dual-value-arbitration",
  "paradigm": "dlpfc-self-control-modulation",
  "name": "Executive Resource ODE with Dual-Value Arbitration",
  "description": "Continuous-time ODE governing depletable executive (dlPFC) resource dynamics, combined with a dual-system architecture where an impulsive controller (taste-driven, Pavlovian/habitual) competes with a goal-directed controller (health-integrated). An arbitration weight derived from executive capacity determines the mixture proportion. When executive resources are critically low, the agent suffers complete self-control failure and is forced to follow the impulsive system. Habit values are learned via model-free temporal difference updating. Based on Rangel et al. (2008) three-controller architecture and Rangel (2013) postulates P2 and P4. Since the environment only provides 'palatability' on food resources, health is derived as (1 - palatability), creating the taste-health conflict that drives dual-system arbitration.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "E",
      "name": "executive_capacity",
      "description": "Depletable dlPFC resource level. 1 = full capacity, 0 = exhausted. Governs the agent's ability to engage goal-directed control over impulsive responses.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 1.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V_I",
      "name": "impulsive_value",
      "description": "Value of action a in state s from the Pavlovian/habitual controller. Driven purely by taste (palatability), distance-discounted. Updated via model-free TD learning.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V_G",
      "name": "goal_directed_value",
      "description": "Value of action a in state s from the model-based goal-directed controller. Integrates both taste and health attributes with respective weights.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V",
      "name": "integrated_value",
      "description": "Arbitrated mixture of impulsive and goal-directed action values, weighted by omega.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "omega",
      "name": "arbitration_weight",
      "description": "Proportion of goal-directed influence in the integrated value. Derived as E^2 to capture nonlinear collapse of self-control under severe depletion.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 1.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "ctrl",
      "name": "control_exertion_flag",
      "description": "Binary indicator of whether dlPFC is actively overriding the impulsive system this step. Set to 1 when the goal-directed and impulsive systems disagree on the best action.",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": 0,
      "range": [0, 1]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_E",
      "name": "depletion_rate",
      "default": 0.08,
      "source": "Knowledge backbone; cognitive depletion assumption from Rangel (2013)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta_E",
      "name": "recovery_rate",
      "default": 0.04,
      "source": "Knowledge backbone: recovery approximately half depletion rate"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "theta",
      "name": "override_threshold",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Derived from Rangel (2013) P4: minimum executive capacity needed for conflict override"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "epsilon",
      "name": "stochastic_lapse_rate",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard exploration/noise parameter in RL; accounts for motor execution noise"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_Q",
      "name": "habit_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard reinforcement learning literature"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma",
      "name": "distance_discount_factor",
      "default": 0.9,
      "source": "Standard temporal/spatial discounting for approach value in grid environments"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_h_G",
      "name": "health_weight_goal_directed",
      "default": 0.6,
      "source": "Rangel (2013): goal-directed system weights health when engaged"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w_tau_G",
      "name": "taste_weight_goal_directed",
      "default": 0.4,
      "source": "Complement of w_h_G, from Rangel (2013)"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Executive capacity dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE). Recovery drives E toward 1 with rate beta_E; depletion drains E at rate alpha_E whenever ctrl=1 (dlPFC actively overriding impulse). Clamped to [0, 1].",
      "type": "ODE",
      "pseudocode": "# Continuous form: dE/dt = beta_E * (1 - E) - alpha_E * ctrl\n# Discrete Euler step:\nE_new = E + beta_E * (1.0 - E) - alpha_E * ctrl\nE = clamp(E_new, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P2, P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Impulsive action value: Pavlovian/habitual value based purely on taste (palatability) of the nearest food resource accessible via action a, discounted by Manhattan distance. For eat at current position, distance = 0.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a in candidate_actions:\n    target_food = best_food_toward(a, position, visible_foods)\n    if target_food is not None:\n        dist = manhattan_distance(next_pos(a), target_food)\n        V_I[a] = target_food.palatability * gamma ** dist\n    else:\n        V_I[a] = 0.0\n    # If cached habit Q-value exists for (state_hash, a), blend:\n    # V_I[a] = max(V_I[a], Q_habit.get((state_hash, a), 0.0))",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Goal-directed action value: integrates both taste and health attributes with explicit weights, discounted by distance. Health derived as (1 - palatability).",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a in candidate_actions:\n    target_food = best_gd_food_toward(a, position, visible_foods)\n    if target_food is not None:\n        dist = manhattan_distance(next_pos(a), target_food)\n        taste_val = target_food.palatability\n        health_val = 1.0 - target_food.palatability\n        V_G[a] = (w_tau_G * taste_val + w_h_G * health_val) * gamma ** dist\n    else:\n        V_G[a] = 0.0",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Arbitration weight: nonlinear mapping from executive capacity to goal-directed influence. Squaring ensures moderate depletion has small effects but severe depletion rapidly collapses self-control.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "omega = E ** 2",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Integrated value: weighted mixture of goal-directed and impulsive action values.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a:\n    V[a] = omega * V_G[a] + (1.0 - omega) * V_I[a]",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Control exertion detection: conflict flag is 1 when the impulsive-best and goal-directed-best actions disagree.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "best_impulsive = argmax(V_I)\nbest_goal_directed = argmax(V_G)\nctrl = 1 if best_impulsive != best_goal_directed else 0",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Habit value update via model-free TD: after eating, update cached Q-value for the (state, action) pair toward the received taste reward.",
      "type": "discrete_update",
      "pseudocode": "if last_action == 'eat' and successfully_ate:\n    taste_reward = eaten_food_palatability\n    state_key = discretize_state(last_position, nearby_food_config)\n    Q_habit[(state_key, 'eat')] += alpha_Q * (taste_reward - Q_habit[(state_key, 'eat')])",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Agent perceives grid position and visible food resources with palatability. Health is derived as (1 - palatability). Two parallel value systems compute action values: impulsive (taste-only) and goal-directed (taste+health weighted). Conflict between these triggers executive resource depletion. Arbitration weight from executive capacity determines the integrated value. Epsilon-greedy selection over integrated values, with forced impulsive fallback when executive capacity drops below threshold during conflict.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# 1. Parse perception",
      "position = (perception.x, perception.y)",
      "visible_foods = perception.resources.food",
      "food_at_pos = [f for f in visible_foods if f.x == position[0] and f.y == position[1]]",
      "",
      "# 2. If we ate last step, update habit values via R7",
      "if last_action == 'eat' and perception.last_action_result.get('consumed', False):",
      "    update_habit_q(last_state, last_eaten_palatability)  # R7",
      "",
      "# 3. Build candidate actions",
      "candidate_actions = ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right', 'stay']",
      "if food_at_pos:",
      "    candidate_actions.append('eat')",
      "",
      "# 4. Compute impulsive values V_I for all actions via R2",
      "V_I = compute_impulsive_values(candidate_actions, position, visible_foods)  # R2",
      "",
      "# 5. Compute goal-directed values V_G for all actions via R3",
      "V_G = compute_goal_directed_values(candidate_actions, position, visible_foods)  # R3",
      "",
      "# 6. Detect conflict via R6",
      "ctrl = 1 if argmax(V_I) != argmax(V_G) else 0  # R6",
      "",
      "# 7. Update executive capacity via R1",
      "E = clamp(E + beta_E * (1.0 - E) - alpha_E * ctrl, 0.0, 1.0)  # R1",
      "",
      "# 8. Compute arbitration weight via R4",
      "omega = E ** 2  # R4",
      "",
      "# 9. Compute integrated values via R5",
      "V = {a: omega * V_G[a] + (1.0 - omega) * V_I[a] for a in candidate_actions}  # R5",
      "",
      "# 10. Action selection: check for forced impulsive failure first",
      "if E < theta and ctrl == 1:",
      "    # Complete self-control failure per P3: forced impulsive action",
      "    action = argmax(V_I)",
      "elif random() < epsilon:",
      "    # Stochastic lapse: random exploration",
      "    action = random_choice(candidate_actions)",
      "else:",
      "    # Normal greedy selection over integrated values",
      "    action = argmax(V)",
      "",
      "return Action(action)"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception.x, perception.y)",
      "visible_foods": "perception.resources.food  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}",
      "food_at_position": "[f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y]",
      "taste_of_food": "food.palatability  # direct mapping from env property",
      "health_of_food": "1.0 - food.palatability  # derived: inverse palatability proxy",
      "ate_successfully": "perception.last_action_result  # check consumed flag after eat action",
      "grid_dims": "(perception.grid_width, perception.grid_height)",
      "current_step": "perception.step"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect reward (1.0 from env); agent internally distinguishes taste reward (palatability) from health value (1-palatability) for dual-system valuation"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Executive capacity depletes during sustained conflict and recovers during non-conflict periods",
      "test_pseudocode": "place high-palatability and low-palatability foods creating conflict → run 40 steps → assert E < 0.6; then remove conflict foods → run 20 steps → assert E > previous E"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "When E is high, agent integrates health into decisions (prefers balanced or healthy food); when E is low, agent acts impulsively (prefers highest palatability)",
      "test_pseudocode": "set E=1.0 → assert agent sometimes chooses lower palatability food; set E=0.1 → assert agent almost always chooses highest palatability"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Hard self-control failure: when E drops below theta during conflict, agent is forced to take impulsive action",
      "test_pseudocode": "deplete E to 0.2 (below theta=0.3) with conflict present → assert selected action == argmax(V_I) for 10 consecutive trials"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Habit values update via TD learning: repeatedly eating high-palatability food strengthens the habit Q-value for that state-action pair",
      "test_pseudocode": "agent eats food with palatability 0.9 ten times at same location → assert Q_habit for that (state, eat) > 0.5"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Arbitration weight omega exhibits nonlinear collapse: E=0.7 → omega=0.49 (moderate), E=0.3 → omega=0.09 (near-impulsive)",
      "test_pseudocode": "set E=0.7 → assert abs(omega - 0.49) < 0.01; set E=0.3 → assert abs(omega - 0.09) < 0.01"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357",
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561"
  ]
}
