{
  "formulation_id": "collapsing-boundary-ode-accumulators-with-lateral-inhibition",
  "paradigm": "drift-diffusion-model",
  "name": "Collapsing-Boundary ODE Accumulators with Lateral Inhibition",
  "description": "Implements a biologically-inspired leaky competing accumulator (LCA) model with coupled ODEs, lateral inhibition between accumulators, and a time-dependent urgency signal that collapses the effective decision threshold. Based on Usher & McClelland (2001) and the collapsing-boundary extension described by Bogacz et al. (2006) and Gold & Shadlen (2007). Each action has a neural-like activation variable that receives perceptual input drive, leaks over time (self-decay), and is inhibited by competing accumulators. A linearly ramping urgency signal progressively lowers the decision threshold, ensuring the agent commits to an action under time pressure. The first accumulator to cross the collapsing threshold wins. This formulation captures postulates P1 (evidence accumulation to threshold), P3 (speed-accuracy trade-off via boundary), and P6 (neural ramp-to-threshold dynamics), and uniquely features inter-action competition and urgency gating absent in Formulations 1 and 2.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "x_i",
      "name": "accumulator_activation",
      "description": "Neural-like firing rate / activation level representing accumulated evidence for action i. Starts at 0 (resting state) and evolves via leaky competing accumulator dynamics. One per action (6 total).",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "v_i",
      "name": "input_drive",
      "description": "Perceptual evidence signal (drift) for action i, combining proximity-based signal and learned reward trace.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-5.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "theta_t",
      "name": "effective_threshold",
      "description": "Time-dependent decision threshold that decreases from initial value 'a' toward theta_min as urgency increases.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 1.5,
      "range": [0.3, 1.5]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "u_t",
      "name": "urgency_signal",
      "description": "Linearly ramping urgency signal that drives the threshold collapse. Represents increasing pressure to commit to a decision.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "R",
      "name": "chosen_action",
      "description": "The action whose accumulator first crosses the collapsing threshold theta(t), or the argmax accumulator at timeout.",
      "type": "str",
      "initial_value": "stay",
      "range": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_bar_i",
      "name": "reward_trace",
      "description": "Learned reward trace for action i, updated via temporal-difference rule after each step. Contributes to input drive.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-5.0, 5.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "s_i",
      "name": "perceptual_signal",
      "description": "Raw perceptual signal for action i derived from resource proximity or resource presence.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "a",
      "name": "initial_boundary",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Ratcliff & McKoon (2008); initial decision threshold before urgency collapse"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "kappa",
      "name": "leak_rate",
      "default": 0.15,
      "source": "Usher & McClelland (2001); self-decay prevents runaway activation"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "w",
      "name": "lateral_inhibition_weight",
      "default": 0.05,
      "source": "Usher & McClelland (2001); competition strength between accumulators"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma",
      "name": "noise_intensity",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard scaling convention; Ratcliff (1978)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "dt",
      "name": "integration_timestep",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Euler discretization step for ODE integration; Bogacz et al. (2006)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "T_max",
      "name": "max_deliberation_steps",
      "default": 100,
      "source": "Prevents indefinite deliberation; practical computational bound"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu",
      "name": "urgency_rate",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Controls speed of threshold collapse; Bogacz et al. (2006) collapsing-boundary extension"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "theta_min",
      "name": "minimum_threshold",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Floor for collapsing boundary (approx 0.2 * a); prevents collapse to zero"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "k_v",
      "name": "drift_scaling",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Maps perceptual proximity signal to input drive units; linked to postulate P2"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha",
      "name": "reward_trace_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.1,
      "source": "Standard RL learning rate for reward trace updates"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Input drive computation: combines scaled perceptual signal with learned reward trace to produce the input current for each accumulator.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action i:\n  v_i = k_v * s_i + r_bar_i",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Leaky competing accumulator dynamics: Euler-discretized ODE with leak (self-decay), mutual lateral inhibition from other accumulators, and additive Gaussian noise. Activations are rectified to stay non-negative.",
      "type": "ODE_euler",
      "pseudocode": "sum_others = sum(x_j for j != i)\nx_i_new = x_i + dt * (v_i - kappa * x_i - w * sum_others) + sigma * sqrt(dt) * random_normal(0, 1)\nx_i = max(0.0, x_i_new)  # rectification",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P6"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Urgency signal: linearly increasing pressure signal that grows with deliberation time.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "u_t = mu * t",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Collapsing effective boundary: the decision threshold decreases from initial value 'a' as urgency increases, with a minimum floor theta_min to prevent trivial decisions.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "theta_t = max(a - u_t, theta_min)",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Boundary-crossing decision: the first accumulator to reach or exceed the time-varying threshold theta(t) determines the chosen action. Ties broken randomly.",
      "type": "decision",
      "pseudocode": "for t in range(1, T_max + 1):\n  compute u_t via R3, theta_t via R4\n  update all x_i via R2\n  winners = [i for i in actions if x_i >= theta_t]\n  if winners:\n    R = random_choice(winners)\n    break",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Timeout fallback: if no accumulator crosses the collapsing boundary within T_max steps, select the action with the highest activation.",
      "type": "decision",
      "pseudocode": "if no crossing by T_max:\n  R = argmax_i(x_i at T_max)",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Reward trace update: temporal-difference update for the chosen action's reward trace after observing the environment reward.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "r_bar_R = r_bar_R + alpha * (reward_observed - r_bar_R)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Per-step decision: compute perceptual signals, form input drives with reward traces, run coupled ODE accumulators with leak and lateral inhibition under a collapsing threshold, and return the first action to cross the threshold. This is the most biologically realistic formulation, featuring neural-like competition (lateral inhibition), habituation (leak), and time pressure (urgency). After reward feedback, update the reward trace for the chosen action.",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# Step 1: Parse perception",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_list = perception['resources'].get('food', [])",
      "food_on_cell = any(f['x'] == pos[0] and f['y'] == pos[1] for f in food_list)",
      "grid_w, grid_h = perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height']",
      "",
      "# Step 2: Compute perceptual signals s_i for each action",
      "actions = ['move_up', 'move_down', 'move_left', 'move_right', 'stay', 'eat']",
      "direction_deltas = {'move_up': (0,-1), 'move_down': (0,1), 'move_left': (-1,0), 'move_right': (1,0)}",
      "for each action i:",
      "  if i in direction_deltas:",
      "    dx, dy = direction_deltas[i]",
      "    neighbor = (pos[0]+dx, pos[1]+dy)",
      "    d_i = min Manhattan distance from neighbor to any food (large number if no food)",
      "    s_i = 1.0 / (1.0 + d_i)",
      "  elif i == 'eat':",
      "    s_i = 1.0 if food_on_cell else -0.5",
      "  elif i == 'stay':",
      "    s_i = 0.0",
      "",
      "# Step 3: Compute input drives via R1",
      "v_i = k_v * s_i + r_bar[i]  # for each action i",
      "",
      "# Step 4: Initialize accumulators at neural resting state",
      "x = {i: 0.0 for i in actions}",
      "",
      "# Step 5: Run coupled ODE race with collapsing boundary",
      "chosen = None",
      "for t in range(1, T_max + 1):",
      "  # Compute urgency and threshold (R3, R4)",
      "  u_t = mu * t",
      "  theta_t = max(a - u_t, theta_min)",
      "",
      "  # Update each accumulator via leaky competing dynamics (R2)",
      "  x_new = {}",
      "  for i in actions:",
      "    sum_others = sum(x[j] for j in actions if j != i)",
      "    x_new[i] = x[i] + dt * (v_i - kappa * x[i] - w * sum_others) + sigma * sqrt(dt) * random_normal()",
      "    x_new[i] = max(0.0, x_new[i])  # rectify",
      "  x = x_new",
      "",
      "  # Check boundary crossing (R5)",
      "  winners = [i for i in actions if x[i] >= theta_t]",
      "  if winners:",
      "    chosen = random_choice(winners)",
      "    break",
      "",
      "# Step 6: Timeout fallback (R6)",
      "if chosen is None:",
      "  chosen = argmax over actions of x[i]",
      "",
      "# Step 7: Return chosen action",
      "return Action(chosen)",
      "",
      "# Step 8 (in update method): Update reward trace via R7",
      "r_bar[chosen] = r_bar[chosen] + alpha * (reward - r_bar[chosen])"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food']",
      "food_on_cell": "any(f['x'] == x and f['y'] == y for f in perception['resources']['food'])",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "step_number": "perception['step']"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect with reward 1.0; move/stay actions yield reward 0.0"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Agent navigates toward food: input drives for movement actions toward nearby food are higher, causing their accumulators to ramp faster and win the race.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place food at (5,5), agent at (0,0) → over 50 steps, assert agent distance to food decreases on average"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Agent eats when on food: the eat action receives s_eat = 1.0 when food is on cell, giving it the strongest input drive and making its accumulator win quickly.",
      "test_pseudocode": "place agent on food cell → run decide() 100 times → assert eat is chosen > 60% of the time"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Lateral inhibition creates winner-take-all competition: once one accumulator leads, it suppresses others, leading to more decisive action selection than independent accumulators.",
      "test_pseudocode": "compare entropy of action distribution vs Formulation 1 under identical drift rates → lower entropy (more decisive)"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Collapsing boundary forces timely decisions: as urgency increases, theta(t) decreases, making it easier for any accumulator to cross, preventing indefinite deliberation in ambiguous situations.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set all v_i equal (ambiguous) → assert decision is made before T_max in >95% of trials"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Leak prevents runaway activation: the kappa * x_i decay term ensures activations don't grow without bound even with high input drive.",
      "test_pseudocode": "set very high v_i for one action → assert x_i stabilizes below a finite value (approx v_i / kappa)"
    },
    {
      "id": "B6",
      "description": "Reward traces bias future decisions: after repeatedly receiving eat rewards, r_bar_eat increases, giving eat a persistent input drive advantage.",
      "test_pseudocode": "give 10 eat rewards → assert r_bar['eat'] > 0.5 and eat is chosen more frequently when on food"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Ratcliff, R. (1978). A theory of memory retrieval. Psychological Review, 85(2), 59-108. DOI: 10.1037/0033-295X.85.2.59",
    "Ratcliff, R., & McKoon, G. (2008). The diffusion decision model: Theory and data for two-choice decision tasks. Neural Computation, 20(4), 873-922. DOI: 10.1162/neco.2008.12-06-420",
    "Bogacz, R., Brown, E., Moehlis, J., Holmes, P., & Cohen, J. D. (2006). The physics of optimal decision making. Psychological Review, 113(4), 700-765. DOI: 10.1037/0033-295X.113.4.700",
    "Gold, J. I., & Shadlen, M. N. (2007). The neural basis of decision making. Annual Review of Neuroscience, 30, 535-574. DOI: 10.1146/annurev.neuro.29.051605.113038",
    "Usher, M., & McClelland, J. L. (2001). The time course of perceptual choice: The leaky, competing accumulator model. Psychological Review, 108(3), 550-592. DOI: 10.1037/0033-295X.108.3.550"
  ]
}

metadata.output: Written 14264 chars to reasoner/drift-diffusion-model/collapsing-boundary-ode-accumulators-with-lateral-inhibition.json

args.content:
{
  "formulation_id": "uncertainty-based-bayesian-arbitration",
  "paradigm": "goal-directed-vs-habitual-control",
  "name": "Uncertainty-Based Bayesian Arbitration",
  "description": "Probabilistic dual-system reinforcement learning agent where both the model-free (habitual) and model-based (goal-directed) systems maintain Gaussian posterior beliefs over action values. The key differentiator from Formulation 1 is that arbitration is driven by relative uncertainty (inverse variance / precision) rather than a fixed training-count decay. When the model-free system has higher uncertainty than the model-based system, goal-directed control dominates (omega → 1); when model-free uncertainty is lower (well-practiced actions), habitual control dominates (omega → 0). This produces adaptive arbitration: the agent can re-engage goal-directed control even in familiar environments if conditions change and model-free predictions become unreliable. The model-free system uses Kalman-filter-style Bayesian updates; the model-based system computes value means and variances from the learned transition model. Based on Rangel, Camerer & Montague (2008) and Rangel (2013), postulates P1–P5.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "s",
      "name": "state",
      "description": "Agent's current grid cell encoded as (x, y) tuple",
      "type": "discrete",
      "initial_value": "from perception (x, y)",
      "range": ["(0,0)", "(grid_width-1, grid_height-1)"]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu_MF",
      "name": "model_free_value_mean",
      "description": "Posterior mean of the habitual action value for each (state, action) pair. Updated via Kalman-filter-style Bayesian TD updates. Represents the agent's best estimate of cached action value.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma2_MF",
      "name": "model_free_value_variance",
      "description": "Posterior variance (uncertainty) of the habitual action value for each (state, action) pair. Starts at sigma2_0 (high uncertainty) and shrinks with experience via Kalman updates. Drives arbitration: lower variance = higher MF reliability.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 1.0,
      "range": [0.001, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu_MB",
      "name": "model_based_value_mean",
      "description": "Posterior mean of the goal-directed action value, computed prospectively each step from the learned transition model and current hunger-modulated desirability. Recomputed every decision step.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma2_MB",
      "name": "model_based_value_variance",
      "description": "Uncertainty of the goal-directed action value, computed from outcome variance in the transition model plus observation noise scaled by experience count. Decreases as the transition model becomes more certain.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 1.0,
      "range": [0.001, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "omega",
      "name": "arbitration_weight",
      "description": "Reliability-weighted proportion of goal-directed control. Computed per (state, action) via sigmoid of the difference in uncertainties: omega = sigmoid(kappa * (sigma2_MF - sigma2_MB)). When MF uncertainty > MB uncertainty, omega → 1 (favor GD). Adaptive and reversible.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu_hat",
      "name": "fused_value_estimate",
      "description": "Precision-weighted mean of model-based and model-free value estimates. Used as the final action value for softmax selection.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-10.0, 10.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "h",
      "name": "hunger_drive",
      "description": "Internal physiological state representing current need. Modulates goal-directed outcome desirability. Rises each step, drops upon consuming food.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_D",
      "name": "decision_time_desirability",
      "description": "Current subjective value of each possible outcome, modulated by hunger. Food: r_D = h * r_food. Non-food: r_D = c_step.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [-1.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "p_hat",
      "name": "learned_transition_model",
      "description": "Agent's estimate of action-outcome contingency p_hat(o|a,s). Updated incrementally from observations.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "N_MB",
      "name": "model_based_experience_count",
      "description": "Number of times each (state, action) pair has been experienced, used to scale down model-based observation noise. Higher count = lower MB uncertainty.",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": 0,
      "range": [0, "infinity"]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "sigma2_0",
      "name": "prior_variance",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Uninformative prior; standard Bayesian initialization for both systems"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu_0",
      "name": "prior_mean",
      "default": 0.0,
      "source": "Neutral prior — no initial preference for any action"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma2_obs_MF",
      "name": "mf_observation_noise_variance",
      "default": 0.50,
      "source": "Reflects noisy experienced-reward signals in model-free learning (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "sigma2_obs_MB",
      "name": "mb_observation_noise_variance",
      "default": 0.25,
      "source": "Lower noise — model-based uses structured knowledge and is inherently less noisy (Rangel, 2013)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_T",
      "name": "transition_model_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.20,
      "source": "Model-based systems update faster (Rangel, 2013; postulate P2)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "kappa",
      "name": "arbitration_sigmoid_sensitivity",
      "default": 3.0,
      "source": "Controls steepness of sigmoid arbitration; tuned for gradual transitions between control modes"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "beta",
      "name": "inverse_temperature",
      "default": 5.0,
      "source": "Moderate exploitation level (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "gamma",
      "name": "temporal_discount_factor",
      "default": 0.95,
      "source": "Standard RL discounting (Rangel, Camerer & Montague, 2008)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "eta",
      "name": "hunger_rise_rate",
      "default": 0.02,
      "source": "Per-step hunger increase when not eating"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "phi",
      "name": "satiation_amount",
      "default": 0.30,
      "source": "Hunger reduction upon eating"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_food",
      "name": "base_food_reward",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Normalized reward unit matching env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_step",
      "name": "step_cost",
      "default": -0.01,
      "source": "Small penalty to encourage efficiency"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Hunger dynamics: hunger rises each step by eta and drops by phi when food is consumed. Clipped to [0, 1].",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "h = clip(h + eta - phi * ate_food, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "Assumption: state-dependent desirability; internal hunger modulates goal-directed valuation"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Decision-time desirability: subjective value of food outcome is hunger-modulated; non-food outcome incurs step cost.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "r_D_food = h * r_food\nr_D_nofood = c_step",
      "source_postulate": "P2 — goal-directed value uses current desirability r^D(o|s)"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Model-based value mean: computed prospectively from learned transition model and current desirability. For eat at food: weighted sum of outcome desirabilities. For movement: step cost plus discounted bootstrap from MF at next state.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a:\n  if a == 'eat' and food_here:\n    mu_MB[s][a] = p_hat.get((s,a,'food'), 0.5) * r_D_food + (1 - p_hat.get((s,a,'food'), 0.5)) * r_D_nofood\n  elif a in ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right']:\n    s_next = clamp_to_grid(apply_move(s, a))\n    mu_MB[s][a] = c_step + gamma * max(mu_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)\n  else:\n    mu_MB[s][a] = c_step",
      "source_postulate": "P2 — V_GD(a|s) = sum_o p(o|a,s) * r^D(o|s)"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Model-based value variance: sum of outcome variance from the transition model plus observation noise scaled down by experience count. Captures both outcome stochasticity and estimation uncertainty.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "for each action a:\n  if a == 'eat' and food_here:\n    p_food = p_hat.get((s,a,'food'), 0.5)\n    outcome_var = p_food * (r_D_food - mu_MB[s][a])**2 + (1 - p_food) * (r_D_nofood - mu_MB[s][a])**2\n    sigma2_MB[s][a] = outcome_var + sigma2_obs_MB / (1 + N_MB.get((s,a), 0))\n  else:\n    sigma2_MB[s][a] = sigma2_obs_MB / (1 + N_MB.get((s,a), 0))",
      "source_postulate": "P2 — model-based system maintains structured knowledge enabling uncertainty quantification"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5a",
      "description": "Model-free Kalman gain: ratio of prior variance to total variance (prior + observation noise). Controls how much the new observation shifts the posterior mean.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "K = sigma2_MF.get((s,a), sigma2_0) / (sigma2_MF.get((s,a), sigma2_0) + sigma2_obs_MF)",
      "source_postulate": "P3 — habitual system updates via incremental experience; Bayesian formulation of TD learning"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5b",
      "description": "Model-free posterior mean update: Kalman-filtered TD update. The 'observation' is the TD target (r_received + gamma * max future MF mean).",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "td_target = r_received + gamma * max(mu_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)\nmu_MF[(s,a)] = mu_MF.get((s,a), 0.0) + K * (td_target - mu_MF.get((s,a), 0.0))",
      "source_postulate": "P3 — V_H(a|s) updated via incremental experience (TD), not decomposed into contingency and desirability"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5c",
      "description": "Model-free posterior variance update: variance shrinks by factor (1-K) after each observation. Captures increasing confidence with experience.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "sigma2_MF[(s,a)] = (1 - K) * sigma2_MF.get((s,a), sigma2_0)",
      "source_postulate": "P3 — habitual system's reliability increases with consistent experience"
    },
    {
      "id": "R6",
      "description": "Transition model update: after observing outcome o_obs from action a in state s, update p_hat toward the observed outcome.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "p_hat[(s,a,o_obs)] = p_hat.get((s,a,o_obs), 0.5) + alpha_T * (1.0 - p_hat.get((s,a,o_obs), 0.5))\nfor o != o_obs:\n  p_hat[(s,a,o)] = p_hat.get((s,a,o), 0.5) + alpha_T * (0.0 - p_hat.get((s,a,o), 0.5))",
      "source_postulate": "P2 — action-outcome contingency p(o|a,s) is learned and updated from experience"
    },
    {
      "id": "R7",
      "description": "Uncertainty-based arbitration weight via sigmoid: when MF variance exceeds MB variance, sigmoid output > 0.5, favoring goal-directed. When MF variance is lower, sigmoid < 0.5, favoring habitual. Kappa controls sensitivity.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "omega[s][a] = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-kappa * (sigma2_MF.get((s,a), sigma2_0) - sigma2_MB.get((s,a), sigma2_0))))",
      "source_postulate": "P1, P4 — two controllers compete; arbitration based on reliability (uncertainty) with adaptive, reversible dynamics"
    },
    {
      "id": "R8",
      "description": "Fused action value: precision-weighted combination of model-based and model-free value means, weighted by the arbitration parameter omega.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "mu_hat[s][a] = omega[s][a] * mu_MB[s][a] + (1 - omega[s][a]) * mu_MF.get((s,a), 0.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P1 — two dissociable valuation systems cooperate via weighted mixing for action selection"
    },
    {
      "id": "R9",
      "description": "Softmax action selection over fused values.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "probs = softmax([beta * mu_hat[s][a] for a in actions])\nselected_action = random_choice(actions, weights=probs)",
      "source_postulate": "Assumption: common currency of value enters common action-selection mechanism"
    },
    {
      "id": "R10",
      "description": "Increment model-based experience count for the executed (state, action) pair.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "N_MB[(s, a)] = N_MB.get((s, a), 0) + 1",
      "source_postulate": "Tracking experience for MB variance scaling"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Each step: (1) Read state s=(x,y), food resources, hunger h. (2) Compute hunger-modulated desirability r_D (R2). (3) For each action, compute model-based mean mu_MB and variance sigma2_MB from transition model (R3, R4). (4) Retrieve model-free posterior means mu_MF and variances sigma2_MF. (5) Compute per-action arbitration weight omega via sigmoid of variance difference (R7). (6) Fuse values into mu_hat (R8). (7) Select action via softmax (R9). (8) After execution: update hunger (R1), transition model (R6), MF posteriors via Kalman update (R5a-R5c), increment N_MB (R10).",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# === DECIDE ===",
      "s = (perception.x, perception.y)",
      "food_positions = [(f['x'], f['y']) for f in perception.resources.get('food', [])]",
      "food_here = s in food_positions",
      "",
      "# R2: Compute desirability",
      "r_D_food = h * r_food",
      "r_D_nofood = c_step",
      "",
      "# R3-R4: Compute model-based mean and variance for each action",
      "for a in actions:",
      "  if a == 'eat' and food_here:",
      "    p_f = p_hat.get((s, a, 'food'), 0.5)",
      "    mu_MB[s][a] = p_f * r_D_food + (1 - p_f) * r_D_nofood",
      "    outcome_var = p_f * (r_D_food - mu_MB[s][a])**2 + (1 - p_f) * (r_D_nofood - mu_MB[s][a])**2",
      "    sigma2_MB[s][a] = outcome_var + sigma2_obs_MB / (1 + N_MB.get((s, a), 0))",
      "  elif a in ['move_up','move_down','move_left','move_right']:",
      "    s_next = clamp_to_grid(apply_move(s, a))",
      "    mu_MB[s][a] = c_step + gamma * max(mu_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)",
      "    sigma2_MB[s][a] = sigma2_obs_MB / (1 + N_MB.get((s, a), 0))",
      "  else:",
      "    mu_MB[s][a] = c_step",
      "    sigma2_MB[s][a] = sigma2_obs_MB / (1 + N_MB.get((s, a), 0))",
      "",
      "# R7: Compute per-action arbitration weight",
      "for a in actions:",
      "  s2_mf = sigma2_MF.get((s, a), sigma2_0)",
      "  s2_mb = sigma2_MB[s][a]",
      "  omega_a = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-kappa * (s2_mf - s2_mb)))",
      "",
      "# R8: Fuse values",
      "  mu_hat_a = omega_a * mu_MB[s][a] + (1 - omega_a) * mu_MF.get((s, a), 0.0)",
      "",
      "# R9: Softmax selection",
      "probs = softmax([beta * mu_hat_a for a in actions])",
      "return random_choice(actions, probs)",
      "",
      "# === UPDATE (after action execution) ===",
      "ate_food = (action == 'eat' and last_action_result.get('consumed', False))",
      "r_received = r_food if ate_food else c_step",
      "",
      "# R1: Update hunger",
      "h = clip(h + eta - phi * int(ate_food), 0.0, 1.0)",
      "",
      "# R6: Update transition model",
      "o_obs = 'food' if ate_food else 'nofood'",
      "p_hat[(s, action, o_obs)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5) + alpha_T * (1.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o_obs), 0.5))",
      "for o in outcomes where o != o_obs:",
      "  p_hat[(s, action, o)] = p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5) + alpha_T * (0.0 - p_hat.get((s, action, o), 0.5))",
      "",
      "# R5a-R5c: Kalman-filter update for model-free posterior",
      "s_next = (new_perception.x, new_perception.y)",
      "K = sigma2_MF.get((s, action), sigma2_0) / (sigma2_MF.get((s, action), sigma2_0) + sigma2_obs_MF)",
      "td_target = r_received + gamma * max(mu_MF.get((s_next, a2), 0.0) for a2 in actions)",
      "mu_MF[(s, action)] = mu_MF.get((s, action), 0.0) + K * (td_target - mu_MF.get((s, action), 0.0))",
      "sigma2_MF[(s, action)] = (1 - K) * sigma2_MF.get((s, action), sigma2_0)",
      "",
      "# R10: Increment MB experience count",
      "N_MB[(s, action)] = N_MB.get((s, action), 0) + 1"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "state_s": "(perception.x, perception.y)",
      "food_positions": "perception.resources.food — list of {x, y, type, palatability} dicts",
      "food_at_current_position": "any(f['x'] == perception.x and f['y'] == perception.y for f in perception.resources.get('food', []))",
      "grid_bounds": "(perception.grid_width, perception.grid_height)",
      "step_number": "perception.step",
      "ate_food": "action was 'eat' and perception.last_action_result indicates consumption"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action at food location → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions → internal step cost c_step = -0.01"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Adaptive goal-directed dominance in novel states: in never-visited states, sigma2_MF is at prior (1.0) and sigma2_MB is also at prior (1.0), so omega ≈ 0.5. As the agent gains MB experience (transition model improves), sigma2_MB drops faster than sigma2_MF initially, pushing omega > 0.5 toward goal-directed control.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Run agent for 20 steps in new area → assert sigma2_MB decreases faster than sigma2_MF for eat-at-food actions → assert omega > 0.5 for those actions"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Uncertainty-driven habit transition: after extensive experience, sigma2_MF shrinks to near zero via Kalman updates. When sigma2_MF < sigma2_MB, omega < 0.5 and habitual values dominate.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Run 300 steps in stable environment → assert sigma2_MF < sigma2_MB for frequently visited (s,a) → assert omega < 0.3"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Reversible arbitration: if the environment changes (food moves), MB predictions update quickly but MF values become stale (high effective error), causing sigma2_MF to effectively increase relative to sigma2_MB, re-engaging goal-directed control.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Train 200 steps → relocate food → assert omega increases for affected states as MF predictions become unreliable"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Devaluation sensitivity in goal-directed mode: when omega > 0.5, reducing hunger immediately lowers mu_MB for eat (since r_D_food = h * r_food), reducing the fused value and suppressing eating.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Set h=0.05 with high omega → assert mu_hat[eat] is low → assert agent avoids eating"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Devaluation insensitivity in habitual mode: when omega < 0.5, mu_MF (which learned from past high-hunger eating) dominates, so reducing hunger does NOT immediately suppress eating.",
      "test_pseudocode": "Set h=0.05 with low omega (overtrained) → assert mu_hat[eat] ≈ mu_MF[eat] (still high from past learning) → agent may still eat"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience, 9, 545–556.",
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724."
  ]
}

{
  "formulation_id": "ode-based-continuous-drive-dynamics-with-deterministic-threshold-policy",
  "paradigm": "pavlovian-control-of-food-approach",
  "name": "ODE-Based Continuous Drive Dynamics with Deterministic Threshold Policy",
  "description": "A continuous-time Pavlovian agent modeled via ordinary differential equations governing hunger drive (logistic growth toward 1, sharp drop on eating) and associative strength V(s) (Rescorla–Wagner learning with explicit extinction decay). The agent computes an effective motivational drive E(t) = hunger × max perceived associative value, and uses a deterministic threshold policy: approach when E exceeds theta_approach (gradient ascent on V landscape), eat when E exceeds theta_eat and food is present, stay otherwise. This produces binary 'released' approach behavior characteristic of Pavlovian responses — the agent either approaches or does not, without graded stochasticity. The ODE framework naturally models temporal hunger accumulation and gradual conditioning/extinction.",
  "variables": [
    {
      "symbol": "H_t",
      "name": "hunger_drive",
      "description": "Continuous internal energy-deficit signal governed by logistic ODE. Grows toward 1 when not eating, drops sharply on food intake.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.5,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V",
      "name": "associative_strength",
      "description": "Dictionary mapping grid cell (x,y) to learned CS–US association strength. Updated via Rescorla–Wagner rule with explicit extinction decay term eta.",
      "type": "dict[tuple[int,int], float]",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "E_t",
      "name": "effective_drive",
      "description": "Combined motivational signal computed as mu * H_t * V_perceived. Compared against thresholds to trigger approach or eating.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 3.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V_perceived",
      "name": "max_perceived_value",
      "description": "Maximum learned Pavlovian value among all visible cells (including current cell and cells with food). Represents the strongest CS signal in the perceptual field.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 2.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_t",
      "name": "reward_received",
      "description": "Scalar food reward obtained at time t. 0 if no food eaten, r_food if food consumed.",
      "type": "float",
      "initial_value": 0.0,
      "range": [0.0, 1.0]
    },
    {
      "symbol": "d_min",
      "name": "nearest_food_distance",
      "description": "Manhattan distance from current cell to closest visible food source. Used for behavioral analysis but not directly in decision equations (approach direction is via value gradient).",
      "type": "int",
      "initial_value": null,
      "range": [0, 18]
    }
  ],
  "parameters": [
    {
      "symbol": "alpha_V",
      "name": "associative_learning_rate",
      "default": 0.10,
      "source": "Within Rescorla–Wagner validated range; Rangel (2013)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "c_H",
      "name": "hunger_growth_rate",
      "default": 0.02,
      "source": "Metabolic hunger accrual per time step; logistic growth toward H=1"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "k_sat",
      "name": "satiation_rate",
      "default": 0.25,
      "source": "Hunger reduction rate upon food intake; calibrated for meaningful satiation from single meal"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "mu",
      "name": "drive_coupling_constant",
      "default": 1.5,
      "source": "Hunger × value multiplicative gain; Berridge as cited in Rangel (2013)"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "theta_approach",
      "name": "approach_threshold",
      "default": 0.3,
      "source": "Effective drive above which approach is triggered; calibrated for typical H and V ranges"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "theta_eat",
      "name": "eat_threshold",
      "default": 0.5,
      "source": "Effective drive above which eating is triggered when food is available; higher than approach threshold"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "eta",
      "name": "extinction_decay_rate",
      "default": 0.01,
      "source": "Rate of V(s) decay during unreinforced CS presentations; slow extinction matches Pavlovian outcome insensitivity"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "r_food",
      "name": "food_reward_magnitude",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Matches env_spec ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "V_max",
      "name": "max_associative_strength",
      "default": 2.0,
      "source": "Asymptote of conditioning; 2x max single-step reward"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "dt",
      "name": "integration_time_step",
      "default": 1.0,
      "source": "Discrete Euler approximation of continuous ODE dynamics; 1 simulation tick per step"
    },
    {
      "symbol": "hunger_override",
      "name": "hunger_override_threshold",
      "default": 0.8,
      "source": "Very high hunger triggers consummatory response regardless of E(t); reflects unconditioned eating (P1)"
    }
  ],
  "rules": [
    {
      "id": "R1",
      "description": "Hunger drive dynamics (Euler-discretized ODE): hunger grows logistically toward 1.0 with rate c_H*(1-H), and drops sharply by k_sat upon eating.",
      "type": "ODE_euler",
      "pseudocode": "ate = 1.0 if (action == 'eat' and eat_succeeded) else 0.0\nH_t = H_t + dt * (c_H * (1.0 - H_t) - k_sat * ate)\nH_t = clip(H_t, 0.0, 1.0)",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R2",
      "description": "Associative strength update (Rescorla–Wagner with explicit extinction): when at state s, V(s) is driven toward r_t if rewarded; V(s) decays by eta when unrewarded. States not visited are unchanged.",
      "type": "update",
      "pseudocode": "pos = (x, y)\nif r_t > 0:\n  # Reinforcement: drive V toward reward\n  V[pos] = clip(V.get(pos, 0.0) + alpha_V * (r_t - V.get(pos, 0.0)), 0.0, V_max)\nelse:\n  # Extinction: gradual decay\n  V[pos] = clip(V.get(pos, 0.0) - eta * V.get(pos, 0.0), 0.0, V_max)",
      "source_postulate": "P2"
    },
    {
      "id": "R3",
      "description": "Maximum perceived Pavlovian value: scan all visible cells (cells with food resources and current cell) and take the maximum V value as V_perceived.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "visible_cells = set()\nvisible_cells.add((x, y))  # current cell always visible\nfor f in food_list:\n  visible_cells.add((f['x'], f['y']))\n# Also consider neighboring cells for movement decisions\nfor nx, ny in neighbors(x, y, grid_w, grid_h):\n  visible_cells.add((nx, ny))\nV_perceived = max(V.get(cell, 0.0) for cell in visible_cells) if visible_cells else 0.0",
      "source_postulate": "P1"
    },
    {
      "id": "R4",
      "description": "Effective motivational drive: multiplicative coupling of hunger and perceived associative value.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "E_t = mu * H_t * V_perceived",
      "source_postulate": "P4"
    },
    {
      "id": "R5",
      "description": "Approach direction via gradient ascent on V landscape: move toward the neighboring cell with the highest V value. Ties broken randomly.",
      "type": "algebraic",
      "pseudocode": "neighbor_vals = {}\nfor action_name, (dx, dy) in move_deltas.items():\n  nx, ny = x + dx, y + dy\n  if 0 <= nx < grid_w and 0 <= ny < grid_h:\n    neighbor_vals[action_name] = V.get((nx, ny), 0.0)\nbest_val = max(neighbor_vals.values()) if neighbor_vals else -1\nbest_actions = [a for a, v in neighbor_vals.items() if v == best_val]\napproach_action = random_choice(best_actions)",
      "source_postulate": "P3"
    }
  ],
  "decision_logic": {
    "description": "Deterministic threshold-based Pavlovian policy: the agent computes effective drive E(t) from hunger and perceived CS value, then applies a hierarchy of rules. Rule A (eat) fires when food is present and drive exceeds eat threshold. Rule B (approach) fires when drive exceeds approach threshold — the agent moves via gradient ascent on V. Rule C (stay) fires when drive is below approach threshold. Rule D (hunger override) forces eating at very high hunger regardless of conditioning. This produces binary 'released' approach behavior — the agent either approaches or stays — capturing the stereotyped, all-or-nothing character of Pavlovian responding (P1).",
    "pseudocode": [
      "# 1. PERCEIVE",
      "pos = (perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_list = perception['resources'].get('food', [])",
      "food_positions = set((f['x'], f['y']) for f in food_list)",
      "food_at_position = pos in food_positions",
      "grid_w, grid_h = perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height']",
      "",
      "# 2. COMPUTE V_perceived (R3)",
      "visible_cells = set()",
      "visible_cells.add(pos)",
      "for f in food_list:",
      "  visible_cells.add((f['x'], f['y']))",
      "move_deltas = {",
      "  'move_up': (0, -1), 'move_down': (0, 1),",
      "  'move_left': (-1, 0), 'move_right': (1, 0)",
      "}",
      "for act, (ddx, ddy) in move_deltas.items():",
      "  nx, ny = pos[0]+ddx, pos[1]+ddy",
      "  if 0 <= nx < grid_w and 0 <= ny < grid_h:",
      "    visible_cells.add((nx, ny))",
      "V_perceived = max(V.get(c, 0.0) for c in visible_cells) if visible_cells else 0.0",
      "",
      "# 3. COMPUTE EFFECTIVE DRIVE (R4)",
      "E_t = mu * H_t * V_perceived",
      "",
      "# 4. COMPUTE APPROACH DIRECTION (R5)",
      "neighbor_vals = {}",
      "for action_name, (ddx, ddy) in move_deltas.items():",
      "  nx, ny = pos[0]+ddx, pos[1]+ddy",
      "  if 0 <= nx < grid_w and 0 <= ny < grid_h:",
      "    neighbor_vals[action_name] = V.get((nx, ny), 0.0)",
      "if neighbor_vals:",
      "  best_val = max(neighbor_vals.values())",
      "  best_actions = [a for a, v in neighbor_vals.items() if v == best_val]",
      "  approach_action = random_choice(best_actions)",
      "else:",
      "  approach_action = 'stay'",
      "",
      "# 5. DECISION RULES (deterministic threshold hierarchy)",
      "# Rule D: Hunger override — very high hunger triggers eating unconditionally",
      "if food_at_position and H_t > hunger_override:",
      "  return Action('eat')",
      "",
      "# Rule A: Eat if food present and effective drive exceeds eat threshold",
      "if food_at_position and E_t >= theta_eat:",
      "  return Action('eat')",
      "",
      "# Rule B: Approach if effective drive exceeds approach threshold",
      "if E_t >= theta_approach:",
      "  return Action(approach_action)",
      "",
      "# Rule C: Stay — insufficient motivational drive",
      "return Action('stay')"
    ]
  },
  "env_mapping": {
    "perception_to_variables": {
      "position": "(perception['x'], perception['y'])",
      "food_sources": "perception['resources']['food'] — list of dicts with 'x', 'y', 'type', 'palatability'",
      "food_at_position": "any(f['x'] == perception['x'] and f['y'] == perception['y'] for f in perception['resources']['food'])",
      "grid_dimensions": "(perception['grid_width'], perception['grid_height'])",
      "ate_food": "last_action_result — check if eat action succeeded (consumed == true)",
      "reward_received": "reward argument in update() method; 1.0 from ConsumeEffect on successful eat",
      "nearest_food_distance": "min(abs(f['x']-x) + abs(f['y']-y) for f in food_list) if food_list else grid_width + grid_height"
    },
    "actions_used": ["move_up", "move_down", "move_left", "move_right", "stay", "eat"],
    "reward_source": "eat action → ConsumeEffect reward = 1.0; all other actions yield reward = 0.0"
  },
  "expected_behaviors": [
    {
      "id": "B1",
      "description": "Logistic hunger growth: hunger approaches 1.0 asymptotically when not eating, with diminishing rate c_H*(1-H)",
      "test_pseudocode": "run 200 steps without food → assert H_t > 0.95 (approaches 1.0 logistically from 0.5)"
    },
    {
      "id": "B2",
      "description": "Threshold-triggered approach: agent stays idle when E < theta_approach but begins approaching once E crosses the threshold",
      "test_pseudocode": "set H_t=0.1, V_perceived=0.5 → E=0.075 < 0.3 → assert action=='stay'; set H_t=0.5, V_perceived=0.5 → E=0.375 > 0.3 → assert action is a movement"
    },
    {
      "id": "B3",
      "description": "Gradient ascent on V: agent moves toward the neighboring cell with the highest V value",
      "test_pseudocode": "set V[(4,5)]=1.0, V[(6,5)]=0.0, agent at (5,5), E > theta_approach → assert action == 'move_left'"
    },
    {
      "id": "B4",
      "description": "Explicit extinction: V(s) decays when agent visits a cell without receiving reward, at rate eta*V(s)",
      "test_pseudocode": "set V[(5,5)]=1.0, agent visits (5,5) with no food 100 times → assert V[(5,5)] approx 1.0 * (1-0.01)^100 ≈ 0.366"
    },
    {
      "id": "B5",
      "description": "Hunger override: at very high hunger (>0.8), agent eats food regardless of low E(t)",
      "test_pseudocode": "set H_t=0.9, V=all zeros (E=0), food at position → assert action == 'eat'"
    },
    {
      "id": "B6",
      "description": "Eat vs approach threshold separation: agent approaches food from a distance (E > theta_approach) but only eats when drive is strong enough (E > theta_eat or hunger override)",
      "test_pseudocode": "set E=0.4 (> 0.3 but < 0.5), no food at position → assert movement action; set E=0.4, food at position → assert action=='stay' or movement (not eat, since E < theta_eat and H < 0.8)"
    },
    {
      "id": "B7",
      "description": "Conditioning: after repeated eating at a location, V(s) increases, making future approach more likely",
      "test_pseudocode": "agent eats at (5,5) 15 times → assert V[(5,5)] > 0.8; E_t increases → approach triggered from farther away"
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    "Rangel, A., Camerer, C., & Montague, P. R. (2008). A framework for studying the neurobiology of value-based decision making. Nature Reviews Neuroscience. DOI: 10.1038/nrn2357",
    "Rangel, A. (2013). Regulation of dietary choice by the decision-making circuitry. Nature Neuroscience, 16(12), 1717–1724. DOI: 10.1038/nn.3561",
    "Rescorla, R. A., & Wagner, A. R. (1972). A theory of Pavlovian conditioning. As cited in Rangel (2013).",
    "Berridge, K. C., & Robinson, T. E. (2003). Parsing reward. As cited in Rangel (2013) for drive coupling parameter μ."
  ]
}
